### Jenelle — 2018-08-07T20:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-light-at-the-end-of-a-tunnel.21777/post-247854>

(copied and pasted response I made elsewhere on the forum recently)

~ if you want a detailed discussion of what's going on with your labs, I think your best bet is going to be to look into an actual consult with Dr. Kruse. I believe you can do that by clicking on the "contact us" button.

Otherwise, it will be sifting through the giant amount of information available at your fingertips ~ in his book, the forum, and all of the resources you've now gained through Gold membership. Fitting pieces together like a puzzle.

Perhaps he will comment on your labs here ~ perhaps he will not. But in an actual consult 1:1 I imagine you'd get a ton of valuable information about what some of these lab values may mean _in_ _your particular context._

Some of the best tips I can give you while navigating the forum and all of this new information:

1) Pay attention to what you see _Dr. Kruse himself_ say. Do not look at information from other members and assume it is correct and in line with his thinking. Some of it is, some of it is not ~ and you will do well to remember that. When something sparks an idea in your mind, it sounds good to you, and you want to know more about it ~ google whatever it is along with "Jack Kruse" and see what you find.

2) I think almost everyone comes here eager for answers and wanting to make changes right away. There are changes that can be made right away, and cost you nothing. They are foundational to everything else you will learn. Get the basics down before you start diving down other deep rabbit holes. For example: really, what good is trying to learn all of the details about deuterium ~ if you are still staying up way past dark, and sleeping in past sunrise?

3) Keep in mind this is a marathon, not a sprint. It's a journey. This place is different from pretty much everything else you have come across ~ meaning, no one is going to give you a list of foods you can/can't eat, a list of supplements you must take, etc. Seldom will you see someone say "you must do this" and if you do, and it isn't Dr. Kruse ~ question it. No one is going to make promises like, "such and such happens in 30 days!" Things are very individual here ~ as we are all unique individuals. There will be no quick fix. Optimal is not a place where you arrive one day and say, "okay, I'm here!" It's a lifelong commitment to being the best human you can be, and your view of optimal will be forever changing.

### Jenelle — 2018-08-07T22:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-light-at-the-end-of-a-tunnel.21777/post-247862>

Believe it or not ~ the purified water bottles from Aldi are RO and say "BPA-free". Yes, I'm sure there are other things in the water bottles that will kill us. But I've got 3 kids in sports & I am dealing with limited (ha) finances and real life every day here ~ so... that's what we buy for now. \*At home\* and for the majority of the time ~ we have the big 5-gallon hard plastic jugs which are also BPA-free. I was delighted to know that the water we've been refilling with for the past 15 years is RO. Prior to that, we lived in the country and had well water with a RO filter system.

### countingstarsx — 2018-08-08T18:41:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-light-at-the-end-of-a-tunnel.21777/post-247916>

> [PJP71 said:](/goto/post?id=247861)
>
> I remember you mentioned getting water form a reverse osmosis source and [@Joyfun](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21372/) mentioned Berkley Filter (her reply to this thread doesn't show up anymore for some reason?) For simplicity, I was trying to find a refrigerator filter that removes fluoride. No luck as of yet. I suppose I could get bottled water but aren't BPAs a concern?

Hi there! I use a Berkey at home too -- but its not the most optimal solution. From what I have read the only 100% way to get all fluoride out is with RO or distillation. The Berkey uses activated alumina which does remove some fluoride, but in optimal conditions (a specific pH) it only gets out something like 96%. Unfortunately, most tap water isn't in that narrow range of optimal pH level so you are left with an even more reduced percentage of fluoride removal.

When we buy a home we will probably get an RO system installed, but until then I am considering getting a few glass carboys and filling them with RO water at the store.

JanSz recommended some (much appreciated) which I will probably end up using unless I can find some type of other "offical list". > [PJP71 said:](/goto/post?id=247840)
>
> Additional things I thought I would mention:
>
> I practice intermittent fasting 5 days a week. Usually from 6pm to 12pm or later the following day.

> This year I started wearing prescription glasses when reading or using computer. The lenses are anti-blue light but not sure how much they block. Purchased them at Costco.

They probably block the minimum, but they will work to some degree. I had cheap $5 ones I bought on amazon back in Dec 2017. They were "ok" but I could feel the difference between wearing them in the office and not. Then I bought the primal hacker yellow tint glasses for the office and those were wonderful. I recently got the RaOptics ones and they are good too. I like the primal hacker ones better !

Can you change the lighting in your office or workspace to limit LED/Florescents? Do you work near windows or anything? Do you sit in front of a screen all day?

Regarding your fasting, are you eating a big ass breakfast if you are fasting from 6pm to 12noon the next day? That could be a reason why your DHEA is low too. # Thread: Why do we see fires in new 5 G regions have melted cars and green trees?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-see-fires-in-new-5-g-regions-have-melted-cars-and-green-trees.21779/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-06T04:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-see-fires-in-new-5-g-regions-have-melted-cars-and-green-trees.21779/post-247633>

For those asking how trees don't burn with temperatures reaching thousands of degrees. Look to the frequency of induction heating via directed energy.

"The electrical frequency influences the process of induction heating. The desired frequency range is determined by the dimensions of the workpiece, type of material, the arrangement between the object and the coil, and the desired depth of penetration. As the frequency is increased, the penetration of heat is decreased. The normal range of frequency is 500 Hz to 10 kHz, known as medium frequency, and 100 kHz to 2 MHz, called high range. Frequencies in the range of 100 to 450 kHz create comparatively high energy heat that is suitable for rapid heating of small objects, or the skin of large parts. When deep penetration of heat is required, extensive cycles of heating at low frequency are essential, frequency range being 5-30 kHz. The most transfer of energy during the process of induction heating is at high frequency when the depth of current penetration is low. It has been established that the recommended frequency should be such that the depth of penetration should not be more than 1/8 times the diameter of the work piece."

[https://www.brighthubengineering.com/hvac/95097-frequency-and-induction-heating/](https://www.brighthubengineering.com/hvac/95097-frequency-and-induction-heating/)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Evening routine to make dozing off easier

**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Forest — 2018-08-06T12:12:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-247645>

there's gotta be a to make this easier

### drezy — 2018-08-06T17:06:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-247692>

Immersion in 55F water around 2 hrs before bed
Direct sunset watching
No lights or eating after sunset
Direct Sunrise sun on skin and eyes

Rinse and repeat for 45-60 days

### Danco3636 — 2018-08-11T22:51:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-248246>

All the the things I do from sunrise until mid day in optimizing my quilt makes me feel like a sleep tank hits me shortly after sunset.

Morning am sun to the max and am Movement along with seafood and connecting the dots sets your sleepy ness at night.

### Lahelada — 2018-08-11T23:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-248247>

The morning sun routine makes the evening routine easier. No blue light at night. Leptin Rx protocol!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-11T23:13:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-248248>

sleep makes optimal easier. stop posting at night.

### Inger — 2018-08-12T10:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-248292>

In addition to the above/morning sun/outside time, just go with the dusk. Do not put on any lights at all, not even a candle. Watch the darkening sky and the stars coming with no glass in between. Very peaceful, very easy. You do not need to do anything. Makes sleepy

I some days have to work later so it is dark as I come home. Not good I know but what I do is immediately as work stops I put on blueblockers, drive home with them on, and enter my dark home, take a shower in the dark and go to bed.
When it is still light out when I come home I do the same, I shower, go to bed and watch the darkening sky from my wide open window and I have no issues falling to sleep at all.....
Pre bed time is for me like meditation time... I love to just.... be.

In winter as it gets dark early here in the north, I just have 1 candle on, and I read. I go to bed earlier in winter and sleep longer. I love that too. It is so healing. This winter I am planning to do some days with no candle at all, just in the dark. I need to try it. I bet it is super healing!

### Sun Disciple — 2018-08-16T20:29:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-248612>

1\. Watching Sunset
2\. Blue blockers
3\. Getting into blacked out bedroom
4\. Buteyko breathing 5 mins
5\. meditation 10 mins

### freddy bee — 2018-09-19T18:06:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evening-routine-to-make-dozing-off-easier.21783/post-251350>

Hot/cold shower 4-5 hours before bed, eating during daylight hours, foam rolling, meditation, night time tea with a touch of honey, etc..

### JanSz — 2018-09-08T19:55:04+0300

For a while I was planning to ask [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) 's take on this topic.

# Thread: Bulk oysters (90lb) in your area without shipping costs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 8

### Forest — 2018-08-06T19:58:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-247709>

like a bag of it

Looking for it in the Durham nc area. Hopefully like a wholesaler thats already got them shipped so you don't have to
How long will it be before they go bad
Is this even a good idea

Buying them for like 1.20 each for blue point gets pretty expensive. Been buying them individually for like 6 months now and got this wild idea. Also which type is best.

### drezy — 2018-08-10T22:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248153>

You'll have to do your own big girl homework on google by searching the terms "durham nc bulk oysters". Mind you that oyster harvesting may be out of season currently and will come back in the fall.

> [Forest said:](/goto/post?id=247709)
>
> How long will it be before they go bad

I've bought 50 at a time and ate them over 10 days and had zero issues. YMMV.

### mitch.moore1 — 2018-08-10T23:02:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248156>

Oysters come with a harvest date tag which will tell you how long the oysters are good for.

### Phosphene — 2018-08-10T23:41:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248158>

I get pints (16 ounces) of fresh, pre-shucked New England oysters for $15 at the local farmer’s market, dated to last 2 weeks but I eat 2 containers per week now. There are about 24 in each plus some juice that I slurp at the end. I could order 100 count live bags for between $60-$90 but then I’d have to store and shuck them myself. This is so easy—just open container, spoon a few out, squirt with lemon and slurp them down with a big glass of water. I do wonder if there’s any DHA quality difference between shucked and unshucked, but not enough to worry about it.

### drezy — 2018-08-10T23:52:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248159>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=248158)
>
> I get pints (16 ounces) of fresh, pre-shucked New England oysters for $15 at the local farmer’s market, dated to last 2 weeks but I eat 2 containers per week now.

Well done! Keep rocking it.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-11T02:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248167>

Joe Patti's pensacola fla. google it during your night time posting tirades

### Forest — 2018-08-11T07:02:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248207>

Yeah gotta change this night owl stuff. Many years of bad habits will take at least 90-120 days. Or maybe just 30. Had a no caffeine pre workout beverage (are those quantum?) it had good ingredients

Seem to have the most creativity and least 'writers block' on night. What about the notion that some h&g (about a quarter) were supposed to be night owls and work the old shift doing the special things like guarding against big cats. Is that just wrong?

If it's from fl, won't the shipping costs for a 90 lb bag be huge (some say it would be like 900). At the same time- how do stores and restaurants get it - it's already been shipped. How did u get them in bulk bags when in Nashville?

### Phosphene — 2018-08-11T17:49:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulk-oysters-90lb-in-your-area-without-shipping-costs.21790/post-248232>

The seafood purveyor at my farmer’s market is also the local restaurant distributor. The fact that he will gladly sell you the same oysters for 1/4 the price charged at restaurants is not advertised. You have to ask. Nicely.

Check out the farmer’s markets and ask around. Asian markets usually have good fresh seafood too. None of those near me unfortunately, but I’d certainly make the hour drive if I didn’t find this convenient option.

I get you on the night owl thing, but it’s amazing how quickly the habit left once I really cut back on evening blue light. Some other poor schmuck will have to guard against the big cats.

# Thread: Playa Del Carmen Megathread

### OptimalSkr — 2018-08-08T16:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-247898>

Hey all so I am finally taking Jacks advice and packing up for Mexico this winter! I plan to go for November to possibly February. I just wanted to start a dedicated thread for the PDC – I know there have been a lot of posts about it in other threads but this will create a library for us folks looking to go there. I think it would be good to catalogue a few things and get advice from others who have been there to make the transition easier on us newbies. Here are a few of the most pertinent topics:

· Best hotels to stay

· Best long term stay locations (monthly rentals) – Areas/Districts of the city that are optimal

· Lowest EMF zones – which types of cell towers they have - where are the good nature areas

· Cenotes List – (have already seen a few of these on the forum)

· Water issues (safe to drink tap water? and wash fruits/veggies etc)

· Where to get high quality groceries (grass fed proteins and organic veggies/fruits etc) and bottled water

· Websites people have used to book deals and find things.

Thanks

### Hunger4More — 2018-09-11T23:53:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-250766>

Just came back from PDC.

Cell towers are everywhere if you stay in the city. Normally, I have a huge issue being close to a cell tower. Once, while on vacation in Dominican Republic, my digestive issues and eczema went crazy as there was a cell tower next to our resort. Even though we were a similar distance to cell towers in PDC, due to the magentic anomaly, I had zero issues.

Cenote jardin del eden was my favourite.

Anything restaurant that's not on 5th ave will be reasonably priced and have local, seasonal food available. Seafood is plentiful. Some of my fav cheap eats were: Los Aguachiles, La Cueva Del Chango, and La Perla Pixan.

### mbhemming — 2018-10-03T21:55:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252272>

Cell towers are everywhere.

It's so easy to make friends there. I wasn't expecting to have a full blown social life within 4 days of being down there. I still skype with two friends I met there. One is from PDC and one from Veracruz.

I stayed about a 5 minute walk from the beach in an AirBnB. Prices of everything are cheaper there. The prices are really cheap if you stay on the other side of the highway bu that may be too far to the beach, unless you have a bike. The place we stayed at cost 80$USD per night split between two people. Everyone told us that we got a good price for Dec 1st to Dec 23rd. I know the members here stay at The Royal. I didn't intend to but I ended up staying close to it and using the beach entrance right beside it. I also played beach volleyball at the resort everyday.

If you stay off the beach then take a taxi home if it's late at night. The police will rob you. They planted drugs on me and I had to give them my money to not go to jail. The actually set up ambush points on side roads and wait for tourists to come. You can walk home if your with a group that has mexicans in it. In the end they only took like 20$ from me lol. It felt like a real trip to mexico after being a victim of extortion!

Even walking 5 minutes away from the beach the food and drinks will be significantly cheaper.

Groceries shouldn't be a problem no matter where you are in the city. But check google maps before booking. I don’t know if the meat was grass fed or anything. Also there is one brand of butter that is real butter. I forget what it was called now.

Taxis will try to rip you off. When you meet mexican friends get them to tell you what prices should generally be.

Don't drink the tap water or order drinks that have ice cubes.

The beach is is nicer in Tutum but it’s nice enough. There is one entrance that smells like shit. Don’t worry, it’s just rotten seaweed. And it’s only at one small area.

If you like to party, then I can tell you about a hostel to go to on wednesday nights. They fill the roof with rooftop parties. It’s open to anyone, you don’t have to be staying in the hostel. Girls drink free and the beers are cheap for guys. I would say it’s 33% local mexicans and 66% tourists. It is the friendliest vibe I have ever seen at a bar.

The main street is made of bricks. You can just walk barefoot and be grounded.

I really loved it there. In fact just typing this message really stimulated feelings of how I mis it. I’m moving to Latin America in December. I plan on being nomadic at first. I hope to spend a significant portion of the year in PDC. I think it would be too hot for me to live there year round. It would probably be harder to get work done too. I plan on mainly living in the altiplano at around 4000-5000ft elevation. It’s like having spring all year round.

### Rubicon — 2018-10-08T18:06:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252632>

The ocean water's fine year round.

The sun is a little underwhelming near Dec 20, but even Nov 15 it still reaches over 50 degrees elevation. The lowest daily maximum it ever really gets down to is 46 degrees. I'd recommend suncalc.org to check that: [https://www.suncalc.org/#/20.6123,-87.0337,6/2018.11.15/12:33/1/0](https://www.suncalc.org/#/20.6123,-87.0337,6/2018.11.15/12:33/1/0)

Right in PDC there is a shallow cenote pool next to the beach that's open to the public for free. I spent time there last November and found it very beneficial. Soaking in cool deuterium depleted cenote water + sun + being in the crater is really great.
Cenote pool as seen on google maps: [https://www.google.com/maps/@20.6486531,-87.0514749,275m/data=!3m1!1e3](https://www.google.com/maps/@20.6486531,-87.0514749,275m/data=!3m1!1e3)

### Nancy Hampel — 2018-10-08T19:04:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252637>

### Rubicon — 2018-10-08T21:37:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252648>

It's a pretty cool spot. You can sit at the spring where water's flowing out of the ground, lay on the sand for some sun, go into the ocean, and repeat.

There's actually a deeper pool as well a few steps back in the jungle that's good for swimming (northwest from the big shallow pool). The deep pool can be seen as the little break in the trees Google Maps.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252657>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=252577)

> I have been following the weather down there and have seen there is lots of rain (October) mostly rain and clouds all days ---- I read that this is the "rainy" season -- but there is probably more UV that comes through the clouds then up here near Michigan now! lol
>
> So you are just chilling in the ocean all day?!!! Cool - ya have seen lots of increases in the cell towers and from reading online the government is hell bent on bringing technology to the Yucatan

Isla mujeres has much less rain than pdc or cancun. quirk of nature.  i've spent about 55 -10 hr. beach days in the last 60 days.

### GringoPerdido — 2018-10-10T03:25:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252778>

If you’re renting a car beware!

Take these notes into account:
[https://www.gettingstamped.com/renting-a-car-in-cancun-mexico/](https://www.gettingstamped.com/renting-a-car-in-cancun-mexico/)

I went with Thrifty. Will let you know how it goes. They seem to have the best reviews on orbitz. Orbitz has free cancelation too.

Also chatted Lloyd and he recommended (if you’re not used to heavy sun):
\- a wide brim safari style hat
\- zinc oxide sunscreen

I want the full benefit without burning too hard.

### GringoPerdido — 2018-10-10T03:28:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252779>

For those worried about montezumas revenge, having some Enterosgel may be helpful. Stops diarrhea fast. Of course the best remedy is avoiding any water that’s unfiltered including salads, ice cubes, etc.

### Gina R — 2018-10-10T06:11:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252781>

Anyone have ideas on a place to stay in PDC? Trying to make it to the member's event this year, but cannot afford the all-inclusive resort that is suggested to stay at...A house share or similar would be great with others looking to go to the event!

### Gina R — 2018-10-10T06:17:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252782>

> [mbhemming said:](/goto/post?id=252272)
>
> Cell towers are everywhere.
>
>
> It's so easy to make friends there. I wasn't expecting to have a full blown social life within 4 days of being down there. I still skype with two friends I met there. One is from PDC and one from Veracruz.
>
>
> I stayed about a 5 minute walk from the beach in an AirBnB. Prices of everything are cheaper there. The prices are really cheap if you stay on the other side of the highway bu that may be too far to the beach, unless you have a bike. The place we stayed at cost 80$USD per night split between two people. Everyone told us that we got a good price for Dec 1st to Dec 23rd. I know the members here stay at The Royal. I didn't intend to but I ended up staying close to it and using the beach entrance right beside it. I also played beach volleyball at the resort everyday.
>
>
> If you stay off the beach then take a taxi home if it's late at night. The police will rob you. They planted drugs on me and I had to give them my money to not go to jail. The actually set up ambush points on side roads and wait for tourists to come. You can walk home if your with a group that has mexicans in it. In the end they only took like 20$ from me lol. It felt like a real trip to mexico after being a victim of extortion!
>
>
> Even walking 5 minutes away from the beach the food and drinks will be significantly cheaper.
>
>
> Groceries shouldn't be a problem no matter where you are in the city. But check google maps before booking. I don’t know if the meat was grass fed or anything. Also there is one brand of butter that is real butter. I forget what it was called now.
>
>
> Taxis will try to rip you off. When you meet mexican friends get them to tell you what prices should generally be.
>
>
> Don't drink the tap water or order drinks that have ice cubes.
>
>
> The beach is is nicer in Tutum but it’s nice enough. There is one entrance that smells like shit. Don’t worry, it’s just rotten seaweed. And it’s only at one small area.
>
>
> If you like to party, then I can tell you about a hostel to go to on wednesday nights. They fill the roof with rooftop parties. It’s open to anyone, you don’t have to be staying in the hostel. Girls drink free and the beers are cheap for guys. I would say it’s 33% local mexicans and 66% tourists. It is the friendliest vibe I have ever seen at a bar.
>
>
> The main street is made of bricks. You can just walk barefoot and be grounded.
>
>
>
> I really loved it there. In fact just typing this message really stimulated feelings of how I mis it. I’m moving to Latin America in December. I plan on being nomadic at first. I hope to spend a significant portion of the year in PDC. I think it would be too hot for me to live there year round. It would probably be harder to get work done too. I plan on mainly living in the altiplano at around 4000-5000ft elevation. It’s like having spring all year round.

Thanks for all that info! Do you mind sharing the name of where you stayed for $80/night?

### mbhemming — 2018-10-11T05:32:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-252865>

[@Gina R](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21555/) The place I stayed at that was 80$ was from Dec 1st to Dec 23. After that it went up to like 250$/night. The time of the members event is the most expensive time. It was a two bedroom.

You can go on AirBnB and look at the prices.

If you want to DM me, I can get you in contact with the landlord. I also know someone else in the building who rents two rooms in her unit. She was charging 30$US per night for a room. I would imagine that price goes up a lot by Dec 24th. It seems like a good rule of thumb is to take the usual December cost and multiply it by 2.5 to get the cost between Xmas and new years.

I recall even hostels were going for 50$/night at that time of year.

On the contrary, when we sweat, we are actually depleting small amounts deuterium. I don't seem to get burnt in the sun generally .. so far .. with the exception of a little around the ankles recently, where the sun had just angled in a little more intensely, and I felt the burn when showering later. So I researched natural sunscreens so I wouldn't be stopped in my sungazing routine the next day (that only filtered a little light) and came up with this .. you can tell me if it sounds like BS, but it said that sesame oil has an SPF of 8, and olive oil has been shown to protect the skin from tumours if applied before going in the sun. I tried the sesame oil, not sure if it would contribute to cooking me .. but it was great. I'm in the sun with no sunglasses, no hat, as little clothing as circumstances allow, grounded .. my skin is fine, so far so good. Family members have had sun-spots/skin cancers burned off, and they are commercial sunscreen wearers. I've mostly never used anything .. my skin is fine .. go figure.

I wanted to ask here on the forum if you gradually develop an ability to look more closely at the sun, or look longer at or around it as time goes on. Or, I was thinking that the skin of the eyelid is so thin that the sunlight would permeate a closed eye just as well?

### GringoPerdido — 2018-10-16T16:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-253389>

This has been a great trip so far.

Definitely come with an unlocked cel phone and get a chip. 200 pesos for unlimited calling and 2gigs of data for like 30 days. 200 for activation.

Ask for your food “sin sal” to avoid the fluoridated salt.

Driving here is easy if you’re used to 3rd world countries. Roads are easy.

I brought a tub of grass fed whey to get early am protein.

Delicious ceviche is available everywhere. I loaded up on homemade yogurt before the trip to build probiotics in my stomach.

### GringoPerdido — 2018-10-21T03:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-253840>

A few updates.

Playa Del Carmen has been my favorite from the places I’ve visited (Cancun, Tulum, Isla Mujeres, and Bacalar). Bacalar is a second and more rural.

Favorite place for a meal and drink: El Boticario - favorite drink is el veneno, great ceviche and raw fish tacos.

Best Ceviche Places: El Oasis and El Pirata.

Lower budget place for drinks and tostadas: Los Hijos Del Mar.

Favorite low price hotel: Casa La Galería - Has a great rooftop patio/pool for sunrise, sunset, and mid day sun bathing.

Favorite coffee shop: Choux Choux

You can get a SIM card for you phone at any chain convenience store. 400 pesos gets activation, installation, 17 days free calling to MX/US and 2gigs of data. They install it and sometimes it takes a few minutes to complete.

I briefly had a bit of Montezumas revenge and used Enterosgel which worked in 2 doses.

Cancun car rental is problematic. They try to up sell crazy insurance. I used thrifty and brought a letter from my credit card benefits program about insurance.

If you plan on using taxicabs here, join the Mexpats Playa or Expats in Playa FB groups and they provide lists of cabs that are fair.

Hope this helps!

IMPORTANT: Hilton bought Royal in last month and changed all policies.
A member has notified us that The Royal no longer honors day passes for their resort, only guests at the resort are allowed on the property. We contacted The Royal to confirm this and it was confirmed. This hotel property policy change was implemented just one month ago. There is no alternative and they will not make any special accommodations for our members that are not staying on the property.
We know that this is terrible news for our members that are not staying on The Royal. This is a policy change that is out of our control.
All meetups and events will happen at The Royal with Dr. Kruse, he will not be traveling outside of the resort. To attend, you will need to be a guest of The Royal (now Hilton Playa Del Carmen).
Team Kruse

### Gina R — 2018-10-25T23:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-254181>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=254178)
>
> I'd avoid Progresso.

Hi [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) , how come you'd avoid Progresso?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-25T23:50:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-254184>

Of the entire crater is has the worse magnetism and water situation. The other end of the crater at Isle of Muerjues is a better choice. The best stretch is between PDC and Tulum.

### Samy Colddigger — 2018-10-30T23:57:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-254592>

> [Gina R said:](/goto/post?id=254586)
>
> Sam, I'm trying to concentrate finding a place as well and want to make sure my time there is spent wisely in the right areas.

Gina, my wife and I are open to sharing accommodation. Maybe in the new year we could share something that is off the beaten path in Isla/PDC/Tulum area? We have a baby however which presents it's own set circumstances. But so far she loves it here!

We are likley going to rent here for November.......in a place called San Benito.....about 25 from Progresso........not sure about local magnetism/water quality but the place is quite isolated so less nnEMF. Right on the beach. Should provide enough time to gauge weather or not it is a good environment for us.

even if its bad magnetism.......we will have sun and ocean and castle to ourselves for very good price!

I think progresso magnetism story is also compounded by power lines. I couldnt figure out what was causing high LF in the apt we are currently even with breakers off. Held meter up to power lines and bingo.....they are bad. Perhaps bc there is shit infrastructure here

Anyways I am going to keep looking and will let you know any updates. If there are powerlines close by then it might be much the same.

Are their shit overhead powerlines in Isla and PDC.....anyone know?

> Just came back from PDC.
>
> Cell towers are everywhere if you stay in the city. Normally, I have a huge issue being close to a cell tower. Once, while on vacation in Dominican Republic, my digestive issues and eczema went crazy as there was a cell tower next to our resort. Even though we were a similar distance to cell towers in PDC, due to the magentic anomaly, I had zero issues.
>
> Cenote jardin del eden was my favourite.
>
> Anything restaurant that's not on 5th ave will be reasonably priced and have local, seasonal food available. Seafood is plentiful. Some of my fav cheap eats were: Los Aguachiles, La Cueva Del Chango, and La Perla Pixan.

Google calls it "Point Esmeralda" (Punta Esmeralda en Espanol). I've seen it referred to as Playa Punta Esmeralda online, but I usually just call it Playa Esmeralda.

Here's a labeled Google Map:

### Sun Disciple — 2018-11-30T18:03:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-257036>

I will be landing In PDC/Tulum area DEC 9th for my annual cenote tour. I will be hitting a different one each day if your in the area between the 9th and 15th feel free to send a message if you wanna hook up.
I also found some interesting timbits here is a mineral analysis of 5 different cenotes around tulum.

[https://www.researchgate.net/figure...nd-rainy-seasons-Values-are-in\_tbl1\_226771719](https://www.researchgate.net/figure/Mean-ionic-salinity-of-the-five-cenotes-during-the-dry-and-rainy-seasons-Values-are-in_tbl1_226771719)

Also I found that Cristal bottled water is sourced from cenote system so it makes a good choice while down there as it should be D depleted.
[http://yucatantoday.com/agua-cristal-2/?lang=en](http://yucatantoday.com/agua-cristal-2/?lang=en)

### Rena3 — 2018-12-27T17:41:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-259068>

It is fine! I feel like the current administration is doing its fair share to strike fear in Americans. Let me put it to you this way- would you go into the sketchier parts of NYC or Chicago and tour around? Border state problems are happening 1000 miles away from the tourist areas of PDC. Use common sense as you would anywhere in a foreign country. Stick to the well- traveled areas and you should be good. We never felt unwelcome or got a side glance. Everyone was lovely. (Then again, we didn’t do any bushwhacking this year, as we have been known to do.)

### Dave Key — 2019-05-10T00:17:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-270073>

Currently, sargassum seaweed is accumulating in significant amounts on Yucatan beaches.
A little research revealed that this seaweed is edible, not the most nutritious but still edible. So instead of cursing it I'm going to start eating it. There's tons of it!

It raises a deeper question, though.
A prehistoric question.
The occlusal surfaces on our teeth resemble the teeth of an herbivore.
Did our ancestors discover DHA-rich seafood while foraging for seaweed once they were separated from jungle canopies?
Could this be a reason why we kept our herbivore teeth, because we traded jungle canopies for underwater canopies; and while harvesting seaweed also discovered how to harvest seafood?

Hey David. Are you in PDC now? I've never been and am thinking of going to stay at The Royal in June or July for a week. Does the seaweed make it impossible to relax and soak in the water? Has Jack ever mentioned any ill effects from soaking in or around the seaweed? Thanks! And do you live there now?

### Dave Key — 2019-05-16T16:40:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-270606>

> [Aaron Bennett said:](/goto/post?id=270592)
>
> Hey David. Are you in PDC now? I've never been and am thinking of going to stay at The Royal in June or July for a week. Does the seaweed make it impossible to relax and soak in the water? Has Jack ever mentioned any ill effects from soaking in or around the seaweed? Thanks! And do you live there now?

The seaweed situation in PDC right now is dismal. The calmness of the waters do not carry away the seaweed so it piles up and accumulates in the water making it muddy and smelly. The only solution is to rent a paddleboard to paddle past the seaweed far enough away from the beach to find clear water to dip in. A guy named Gustavo rents paddle boards by the hour, day, week.
Isla mujeres, the island adjacent to Cancun is free of seaweed at the moment. Cozumel might be another island option to find beaches without seaweed. Further south in Tulum where I am camping on the beach the 2-3 foot waves keep the seaweed from pooling at the shore as it does in the calmer waters of Playa del Carmen.
There are practically no tourists on the beach in Playa. Most visitors are walking up and down the shopping area along 5th avenue.

### Dave Key — 2019-05-18T00:44:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-270719>

There are great cenotes close to Playa.
I spent my first night on Isla last night. The water is cool in the morning and barely any seaweed, beautiful blue water. Playa beaches smell so bad nobody even goes. The seaweed is really bad.
If you stay in Playa you could take an ADO bus up to Cancun and ferry to the Isle, and you can take a Colectivo van down to cenote's. We went to Eden cenote the other day. It was my first cenote trip and I brought flippers, snorkel & mask. It was awesome! It was a great break from the heat.

### Novah — 2019-05-19T23:12:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-270872>

So I’m going to jump in here and say something that may not be popular to say but I wish I’d had this heads up prior to going to the area...

My health got significantly worse in the Yucatan/ PDC coast.

I’ve been to a lot of sunny, warm places where I felt significantly better/great but the Yucatan was not one of those places.

It was disappointing to have the experience I had, because I had high hopes due to all the hoopla around the area. It ended up being a large waste of money and time, and set my health back, in my case.

All I can say is, one size shoe doesn’t fit all nor does one place.

My 2 cents to anyone who is considering the Yucatan for their health is remember that everyone is different; my suggestion would be to go to whatever location in the world you’re drawn to or/and that’s easy for your body — that’s likely a good indication of a good spot for you. And this is just speculation on my part, but if saunas help you feel better, the Yucatan might be a good spot for you. If saunas make you feel worse, the Yucatan may not be a good spot for you. And if you’re not feeling well somewhere, leave, no matter how “healing” a place apparently is. You have to trust your gut and your body.

I really think we need to start addressing this issue on this forum - there are many places to feel better and although some may feel better in the Yucatan, others may feel no change and others may feel worse. And there’s nothing wrong with that, but it does need to start being acknowledged rather than glossed over or ignored.

There’s been a lot of people that have gone to the Yucatan and not seen the gains they were hoping for — and it’s likely because it simply was just not the right place for them.

And for those whom it really helped their health, I’m glad you found your happy healing place

Do you have sufficient evidence to say that compared with the reasoning of "why Yucatan" that is being presented here?

### Novah — 2019-05-20T02:01:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-270882>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=270878)
>
> What did you do when you were there ? I remember you said the same about CT. That made you worse,too. If you are new here maybe it might be an idea to ask what may have happened instead of advising everyone to follow their calling based on your notions of attraction? Do you have sufficient evidence to say that compared with the reasoning of "why Yucatan" that is being presented here?

Hi there,

I've been a Gold member for almost a year now and around longer than that. I had a consult with Dr. JK prior to going to the Yucatan.

I traveled and stayed up and down the coast in various locations, measured with my meter wherever I could and definitely my sleeping locations, and I was there for 5 weeks at which point I had to return home I was feeling so bad. Lots of time in the sun, cenotes, ocean,sunrises/sunsets, lots of time outside etc. Wifi was off at the house, computers were corded etc -- all the stuff we talk about on here. At some point I'll put together a post outlining my experience.

I understand what I'm saying may not be popular to say on here, hence I get the sense you're jumping down my throat a little here about it, but I do believe it does need to be talked about.

Considering I feel great in other warm, sunny locations, for me it was definitely a location issue. This is the key point.

If someone had made mention of what I'm saying prior to my trip, I personally would have really appreciated it.

I didn't say CT made me worse - I can swim in glacier lakes and rivers and tons of time swimming in the ocean (especially if the air is warm). I believe you're referring to this thread where a forum member made mention he was going to stop CT as he thought it was making him worse -- others mentioned it couldn't be the CT, my thoughts were that perhaps the CT wasn't agreeing with him at this time, we're all different and to keep an open mind.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-body-temp-but-warm-hot.23641/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-body-temp-but-warm-hot.23641/)

Hi Caroline,

I'll check out SueUK, thanks for the tip.

It wasn't Mexico per say, I've spent significant amounts of time in a different part of Mexico without issue and large healing benefits. It was the Yucatan that I felt significantly worse in and yes, it would be very interesting to know why I felt worse there.

I did send Jack a detailed email a while back about my experience, as I wanted to let him know first, before I posted here. I didn't hear anything back from him, but he's also a really busy guy.

I did reach out to 'mission control' re: a consult, but didn't hear anything back. If at some point I hear back, then I'd love to have another consult with him and hear his thoughts.

There actually have been quite a few mentions in the last 6 months on here and the Q&As (and there's been rumblings through word of mouth) mentioning that they weren't seeing the gains they were hoping for in that location - what you have to understand is, when I say this, it's not a criticism, but rather, "hey, this place is working really well for some, but not for others, and let's discuss this" and if we do, maybe we can figure out why it's successful for some and not for others, and maybe there's clues so people would know ahead of time whether or not this is a location that might work well for them (or not).

### Phosphene — 2019-05-27T06:40:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-271355>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=270244)
>
> How does everyone recognise Shah when they go to meet him??????????

Funny story. My side trip to Isla Mujeres was not nearly as well planned as my journal may have implied. I had a strong feeling I could make it happen, that I NEEDED to make it happen, but wasn’t 100% sure I could pull it off and part ways with my family at the airport until the very day before. I was a bit unnerved by the fact there was apparently no actual address for the place that fellow mitochondriac [@Inna](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20770/) and I would be staying. (Lloyd, what do you mean there’s no address? So I’m supposed to ask a taxi to take me to Mike’s Pizza if for some reason you can’t meet me at the ferry? And if the driver doesn’t know where that is, ask for Mango Cafe, it’s near there? Really???)

I’m still not sure how I managed to convince my family that I hadn’t truly lost my mind. (Yeah that’s right hon, I’ll be staying with this man I met on a quantum health internet forum, someplace on an island in Mexico, with another forum friend I’d met just once before... Don’t worry—he’s married! I’ll call you when I get there...)

So yeah, somehow I managed to get to Isla, basically on faith and cajones, plus a few phone calls to Lloyd once I figured out the proper number.

Everything went fairly smoothly once the ball was truly in motion. My family wasn’t thrilled but they knew I was set on this, and it actually wasn’t the craziest thing I’d ever done in the pursuit of health.

I cancelled my flight back home, negotiated a shuttle to the ferry, and Lloyd would be meeting me at the island terminal. I really had no clue what he looked like but just assumed we would spot each other instantly—quantum entanglement and all. I knew he was in his 60’s and presumably retired, so naturally I was on the lookout for someone who looked, well, 60-ish. After a few minutes waiting I started to worry, but didn’t panic. Just relax, he’ll show up.

Few more minutes, then a few more. Phone call—no answer, but he’s probably in airplane mode. I approached a couple men who could possibly be him. Lloyd? No sorry....

What the hell??

Then finally with options dwindling I asked this trim guy in his 40’s if there was another area of the terminal where someone could be waiting.... and lo and behold it was Lloyd! The image I had in my head and the person standing there were as far apart as they could be. The deeply burnished tan should’ve been the clue, but the rest of it just didn’t compute!

Lloyd has indeed found the fountain of youth and health, and as he will proclaim to any and all who will listen...

YOU MUST BE IN THE WATER!!!!

MOST OF THE DAY!!!

NEARLY EVERY DAY!!!

FOR MORE THAN A FEW WEEKS!!!

(This picture captured him briefly out of his natural habitat....)

### Phosphene — 2019-05-27T16:12:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-271380>

Also, I don’t mean to belittle the experiences of any like [@Novah](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22945/) who may have had a less- than-healing time in the Yucatán. Travel is stressful under the best of circumstances, and for me staying in one place the whole time was key. Others were wandering all over—a few nights here, a few there, never really settled. I chose to immediately adopt the hardcore mitochondriac habits of both Lloyd and Inna, and I learned a lot and benefited greatly. Plus just being around other humans who don’t think you’re losing your shit is such a mental boost!

Is the apartment on Isla perfectly optimal? No. But the environment is a TON better than any other affordable short-term rental you can find. I chose to overlook the fact that it is literally right next door to an open-air pizza joint and managed to resist both the pizza and the homemade cannolis luring me ever time we got back. Most days I was too wiped out from spending ALL DAY at the beach and IN THE WATER and just retired to my spartan, but cool, dark and quiet bedroom.

Go visit him folks! (Just not mid-January to mid-February—that’s hopefully mine!)

And feel free to ask me any questions.

### Novah — 2019-05-28T23:21:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-271473>

All good Phosphene and thank you

In my case, I rented one place for my entire stay. Because I wasn't feeling well there, I also tried out other towns to see if it made any difference (it didn't) but I always had home base to fall back on so that relieved any travel stress of scrambling to find a new rental.

I agree having one place to stay is a really good idea, but the downside is it may tie you to the wrong place; sometimes it's best to be quick and nimble and able to redirect.

If I had a do over I'd put myself into a direct waterfront (no mangrove) all inclusive for the first few weeks while I found my footing in a new place.

Yes, it was definitely a less than healing experience  It was actually the worst I've ever felt anywhere.

By the end it felt like I was going to end up hospitalized or possibly dead (yep, that bad) and I knew it was time to leave immediately. Within 24 hours of returning home, the worst of my symptoms just stopped. Gone. Strangest thing.

And it's taken a while for my body to recover from that trip, and considering I go to the sun to feel better, feeling worse is not an option I'm looking for in a location.

I pretty much had the flip opposite reaction Deanna had; for me, the Yucatan was like her DC.

And knowing what I know now, and how my body reacted to the area, I wouldn't return to the area. I'll be heading back to my safe, low crime, low population fishing village on the Mexican west coast  and branching out to explore a few others...

So the point of sharing all this is just to act as a heads up - the Yucatan might work wonders for some, and it may cause problems in others, so I think it's wise we take a balanced view of the area - we can't just assume it's going to help everyone. For my particular system, I've had way better healing success elsewhere.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-271540>

Yes, [@shah78](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1152/) is that damn cannoli placard still right outside, taunting?

For some reason I didn’t take pics in or around the apartment—perhaps to preserve some of the mystique.  It’s definitely no-frills but that wasn’t my reason for going. I’d already somewhat over indulged at the parent-gifted all-inclusive resort week, so I tried to treat the Isla week as detox. And a Masters-level tropical mitochondriac crash course.

Oh I do have one pic at the apartment. This is the view out the front door, which opens up almost directly onto the busy (during the day) street. I think this was my first day there so we weren’t at the beach the whole time, and Lloyd generously shared some of his dinner: grass-fed beef sautéed with onion, garlic, ginger, and chunks of fresh turmeric. Plus avocado, sea salt and pepper. Absolutely delish!

My one gripe is that it was harder than anticipated to source good fresh protein. There was a glitch in the delivery order of grass-fed beef, pastured eggs, etc. from the mainland co-op, and the best fresh seafood was being hoarded by the restaurants in high season. (That’s the reason I didn’t cook—there was nothing much to cook besides fruit, veg and chicken at the local market! ) I guess I could’ve checked out the options at the Walmart-equivalent within walking distance, but I was there for fresh local seafood dammit so I mainly ate a few gallons of fresh ceviche from a mom & pop stand. It was such a hardship!

Oh, [@Inna](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20770/) did come up with a mystery fish/veg concoction that we took for breakfast/lunch a few days—that was good too! Loads of raw garlic—no problems with island vampires that week.

For complete honesty I did indulge in two mid-week early dinners out, one as a guest of another visiting mitochondriac and this one, shared with [@Meli](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20876/). Seafood pizza!! (It was all her idea—how are you Melinda?)

Looking back I have to say the experience in the Yucatán FOR ME was healing and downright magical just to finally meet and hang with some fellow mitochondriacs. We all know how lonely and alienating this lifestyle can be. Community is key!!! I felt very safe and looked after with my quantum Sherpa host and my roommate.

My eczema improved by 75% in two weeks, and returned with a vengeance back home. No noticeable improvement in the tinnitus and Eustachian tube dysfunction, but I wouldn’t expect that so quickly. Mood and fatigue improved 1000%!

### Dave Key — 2019-06-05T18:22:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-272076>

Tulum vs. IslaMujeres
There is a great vibe in Tulum. It's very easy to make friends and most of the people I've encountered have a health-centered lifestyle. They each have their own different flavor of "healthy" so we trade health tips and have deep discussions about health issues and remedies. I've been lucky to find more people open to our quantum bias, so I don't feel like I'm wasting my breath on the brainwashed masses.
At first Gina and I were at the beach for sunrise, and every day it seems like more and more people were showing up for the daily rise.
I love this sense of community in Tulum but I'm not necessarily sensing the quantum magnetic effect of the crater to the north. I'm here to heal and Tulum provides great bonding and spiritual entanglement, but I'm heading back up the coast to Isla Mujeres for my body's magnetic needs. Perhaps bouncing between the two areas will balance all my needs.

### Sean Waters — 2019-07-09T17:52:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-273922>

[@Novah](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22945/) ... interesting to have your experience here.

Can you give me some background on your situation?... The common theme for me seems to be RF. And the common theme for those who do "bad" in Mexico seem to be Electro-Hyper Sensitive.

But it's interesting that you go back and feel great. Where do you go back to? Where do you call home? Do you have EHS?

I also struggled in Mexico last year, there for 2 months on Isla with Shah, but some systems did great and others lagged. E.g. My dopamine got so high I could give up a smoking habit after 9 days, but my skin remained itchy during sleep.

I lacked the foresight to know why. I've figured a lot out since then.

I'm heading to that region again next Winter for round 2, and I plan to go for much longer. I also plan to try out Playa/ Tulum. I'm also planning on Columbia and other countries and testing those out too.

I'm aware of the issues I may face, and I also agree that I've heard some people aren't doing so well. **But figuring out why is really important to this forum.**

I've travelled to other countries in the East - and ran into similar problems. People with EHS have wrecked Mito Proteins... perhaps, they need longer and more cooling to tighten those proteins. Akin to putting a huge voltage down an old crappy wire...

There is also the Autophagy/ Apoptosis issue.... the Gut/ Skin recycle fast, but the Brain and CNS takes a long time...

Jack always says 18-36 months. I don't feel like 2 months is enough to make a judgement, but if you were close to death - then that is something else.

You have to make a really good assessment of your Redox. This is usually really hard for someone making that Leap to PDC, often they go because they are desperately low redox and low dopamine.

I discussed with Jason in Poland... how some people have "very reversible" problems, the system is off, but the Mito's aren't actually damaged.

Those with EHS and Brain/ CNS issues can have a very difficult time. We actually discussed the possibility that EHS isn't truly reversible at all...

Bad news for me. I struggle with both the above ^.

But all the more reason to carefully assess **the difference between a Bad Environment and a Bad Redox.**

> I agree having one place to stay is a really good idea, but the downside is it may tie you to the wrong place; sometimes it's best to be quick and nimble and able to redirect.
>
> If I had a do over I'd put myself into a direct waterfront (no mangrove) all inclusive for the first few weeks while I found my footing in a new place.

>
> By the end it felt like I was going to end up hospitalized or possibly dead (yep, that bad) and I knew it was time to leave immediately. Within 24 hours of returning home, the worst of my symptoms just stopped. Gone. Strangest thing.

Shah's Isla bingo.

Contestants get assigned

-   a diving mask
-   long snorkel
-   waterproof laminated bingo card

### caroline — 2019-07-10T13:08:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-273999>

I find it really interesting that friends has moved to #2 on the 6 best healers list.

Friends are so very important in our life but........ what if we are unduly influenced by a trusted friend? A trusted friend - but they do not walk in our shoes.......

I think we need dear and trusted friends absolutely for sure ...... but major decisions are 150% our responsibility.

Do we start to rely on our circle of six too much?

My circle seems to change periodically .... ultimately, I need feedback and discussion and support from people I trust - but the buck stops with me.

How many times have we been disappointed by someone we trusted.....?

Have you ever listened to a friend's advice and then realised you made the wrong decision based on that advise? The decision didn't come from your heart of hearts.....you farmed it out to someone else?

Do you have men and women in your circle? assorted ages?

Actually - I think we would be really lucky to have 6 trusted friends. I only have a couple.

Also - I worry about burdening friends with my issues. I know you need someone to listen at times - but that is a heavy burden for someone else to carry on our behalf.

I don't think I have written this very well....but it is a start. I have been thinking about this for weeks.

### Sean Waters — 2019-07-11T22:02:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-274088>

[@In Voluntate Dei](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23766/)

This is the question we are kind of speculating on here. We know it **should work** but generally people's different context can produce a different result than others. Probably heard Jack talk about Mitochondria - well, they are at the heart of it.

If you have the ability to fully move, and long term, with financial freedom.... then Jack **always always says:** "The stretch between Playa DC and Tulum".... that is, Puerto Venturas and south, all around the areas close to the Cenotes: Eden and Sac Atun and there's many more.

_**But it might not be the best spot for you or your daughter - so you may want to rent somewhere for a few weeks at a time, AirBnB or something and float around and see where you both sleep good.

Find somewhere with low EMF, that is really key.**_

Using these Cenotes daily with Sun is what I'm planning to do come November/ December, for as long as I can or until I feel alright.

But, although I'm going in better health this time, with more knowledge and preparation, I do anticipate a bit of an obstacle course before I start to feel "miraculously cured".

These are things you should take note of, as well as listening to the Q&As from 2018 and 2019, lots of members call in from Mexico and discuss how they are doing/ feeling... it would be a huge benefit to get in touch with them. You can PM me if you need to also, but I'm not there yet.

Shah you've sat there for 4 years. You are retired. You got enough cash to make it last. **Don't speak for others when you say "lots of talking, no fixing". You really have no fucking clue what it takes for someone to get to your position.**

And thank god it works for you... Jesus, sitting in the water on your own staring into space for 12 hours a day in Mexico I'd fucking hope you had reversed 8 problems.

How can you not understand that where you sit might not work for everyone in the same way/ timescale?

**_Don't you know that some people are Uncoupled and do better with Cold? Or that those with EHS need COOLING and your luke warm water in Playa Norte might not be cold enough?_**

You saw me sit with you for 2 months... My Gut got better, but I was **_Still fatigued, still scratching in my sleep, and STILL mentally poor._**

How come I couldn't stay for 4 years?... I ran out of cash. Unfortunately, I can't just sit there with a pension coming in.
**_
Your ignorance is incredible. That's one thing that Isla hasn't helped for you.
_**
We also know from the Q&As that both Jack and Melinda both didn't do good when they visited Isla. I also know others who have been there who haven't shouted and sung it's praises. But then some do ok, and some do good.

IT ALL DEPENDS. THIS IS THE QUESTION.

You can keep getting annoyed all you like - but the reality is this 24 year old girl is Fatigued and from a shitty EMF environment - **_just like I was -_** and she may come to Isla and feel overheated, depressed and not get results - **_just like I did_**.... She has completely different Mitochondria to you. She has spent 25 years of her life with Un-Myelinated Neurons getting fucking battered by nnEMF and Blue Light. Luckily, yours were Myelinated before you picked up a Smartphone or a Computer. So her tolerance to RF is going be very different to yours.

Also, You shouting at her to sit in the water ain't gonna do shit but make her feel even more broken and concerned for her health. #2 Friends is a bigger player than you care to realise... If she is alone on that island or isolated from her friends or people who understand her - how then will her Redox fair?... I certainly didn't feel good without a Support Network/ Circle of 6 and all I wanted to do was reconnect to my people back home.

BTW, that Water you sit in is some of the most polluted with shit an germs in the whole of Mexico. People pull up on Boats to playa norte and leave all their booze, cigars, piss and shit in the water.

When it is Christmas/ High Season the fucking RF there is insane because there are thousands of Cancun tourists sat around on Facebook and Instagram.

I don't actually think you even fully understand your own environment, or yours or anyone elses Mitochondria.

Keep getting angry bro. But, if she is EHS and Fatigued then she may have a real obstacle course before she feels good. OR she may do great. Understanding this is really important. **Don't be ignorant to other people's personal context.**

### Phosphene — 2019-07-12T18:55:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-274179>

> [In Voluntate Dei said:](/goto/post?id=274121)
>
> Could you give me info on where you stayed? I am looking to visit there with my daughter in September/October and I dont mind the pizza and cannolis. It seems to me that the Isla mujers is a quieter place. I need to be close, walking distance to beach. My daughter is very fatiqued and I cannot move around too much with her. Cenotes is definitely out of the question for now.Thanks a bunch!

Hi! I stayed with Lloyd ( [@shah78](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1152/) ) and I’m in the process of finalizing my next winter healing session—this time a month in Mexico, with at least three weeks on Isla (one with my family), and possibly elsewhere visiting a few peeps. I’m calling it my Mexican Redox Redux

Now I have to admit I’m extremely fortunate that my physical and mental health issues are not nearly as severe as what many others are facing. This is entirely due to following Kruse principles (even with my inconsistencies) and a bit of yoga and meditation. I haven’t been to an allopathic or alternative practitioner in over two years, possibly three. I’m losing count.

I’m also fortunate to be semi-retired at age 50, after too many years slogging through a corporate career that very nearly killed me. I’m pretty sure I have a second (actually third) act though—still determining what that needs to be.

I say all this just to provide context. I’m trying to picture what I would do if one of my young adult kids had a mysterious incapacitating illness. I really feel for you and pray I never face the same situation.

Would I recommend you stay with Lloyd? Yes, with a few caveats:

-   His apartment is NOT near the beach, at least not one where you can hang out all day. Taxis are necessary and while cheap and abundant, they tend to favor the local workers so it can mean a short walk (block or two) and wait (up to 15 minutes). You could always grab the taxi and then pick up your daughter.
-   There is a walk from taxi drop to the beach of several hundred yards. You could certainly stop for breaks, but keep that in mind if she is so incapacitated. Depending on conditions (wind, minor seaweed) another short walk could be needed to get to a better spot.
-   There are no public bathrooms on the beaches—only a few porta potties. Was not a problem for me since my morning dumps were on schedule and of course everyone pees in the ocean (right? ) but just something to consider. You can always buy something small at one of the beach cafes and go there.
-   There is relatively little shade, and although that’s of course a positive the sun is indeed intense. The idea is that you wear a broad hat to protect your face and neck and stay submerged but that’s not entirely practical. During the high mid-day UV I needed a few dabs of natural zinc oxide on tender bits till my solar callus (tan) improved.
-   Yes, i suppose it could get a tad tedious with little to do all day besides talk, eat, swim/snorkel or listening to downloaded music/podcasts/webinars but that wasn’t my experience. Lloyd is a fascinating conversationalist but he needs downtime too. I was lucky that several mitochondriacs were visiting that week, and I’m absolutely fine with entertaining myself with just my freaky brain—absolutely no room for boredom!

Okay enough of the possible downsides. (Or at least all I can think of for now. You can search some of my other posts for additional details of my experience.) Overall, Lloyd is a compassionate, knowledgeable, dedicated and trustworthy quantum host. He will no doubt do everything in his power to help your daughter.

A little known fact that I hope he doesn’t mind me sharing is that he’s also an experienced neuro-muscular therapist (masseur who knows his shit) with expert knowledge of anatomy/physiology. I didn’t last year but will definitely this year enlist those services.

Hope this helps!

### Phosphene — 2019-07-12T19:18:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-274182>

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=274135)
>
> BTW, that Water you sit in is some of the most polluted with shit an germs in the whole of Mexico. People pull up on Boats to playa norte and leave all their booze, cigars, piss and shit in the water.

Not my experience, but I’m no water expert either. During my visit in mid-January the water was pristine, with very tolerable amounts of seaweed. But then again I’m relatively easy to please.

Isla has the advantage of being an island of course, so the mild but constant currents sweep excessive seaweed, debris and any nasties away.

After your daughter's not being assaulted 24/7 and can begin recovering there's surely plenty of nature around there she can spend time in to accelerate healing. After she returns closer to 'normal' by having a non-toxic home environment, then you can do as much redox vacationing in the Yucatan or elsewhere as you want.

### shah78 — 2019-07-12T20:53:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-274193>

holbox was much better than Isla Mujeres. No cars allowed. But they have a huge sewage problem. They have a mosquito problem certain months of the year. And there was absolutely no protein Source available on the island outside of a restaurant. They're also no banks. I still would have given it a try but I had better connections for apartments on Isla.

### Sean Waters — 2019-07-13T13:38:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/playa-del-carmen-megathread.21799/post-274229>

It is interesting how I could group most of you into Generation X, and state that all of you have a totally different experience of your environments than those of us from Generation Y....

Generation Y
_noun_
noun: **Generation Y**; plural noun: **Generation Ys**

1.  the generation born in the 1980s and 1990s, comprising primarily the children of the baby boomers and **typically perceived as increasingly familiar with digital and electronic technology.**

\-----------------------------------------------------

I'm also glad none of you have this insuffering impatience to feeling irritable, stressed and bored on a super-hot beach when you got EHS.

I think the struggle comes back to nnEMF and Blue Light exposure to the Central Nervous System which is unmyelinated for the first 25 years... Most of Generation Y has been playing on Video Games and Computers since before that age of 10. That's 15 years of Raw Vinegar poured over Open Flesh............ We've probably also had the least time outside, the most vegetables/ grains and "health" food pushed onto our plates, and the most RF naturally occuring in our environment due to increase of Cell Phones and WiF - even if we didn't sit on Tech all night on Grand Theft Auto.

You can start to build a picture of why we are so sensitive and we have so many issues with the Sun and the Natural Environment. The next generation are really going to have issues...........

Although I state "boredom", that is how I describe it - but in reality, what is boredom? and why do I feel it?... why couldn't I be content?... You are right to be averse to this feeling, it isn't natural.

I think it is a Dopamine based issue. The feeling of satisfaction, reward, motivation to just "be" on this beautiful beach.

Again, that is something Generation Y really can't vibe with......... they have to take a pic on Instagram to enjoy it, get some likes rolling in for them to truly appreciate it. For me, it was a time when leaving London after 2 years - my Redox was the worst it had EVER been.... and my Dopamine was in a hole...... based on me smoking cigarettes and boozing before I left in depression, and that was probably, due to 20 years of Microwaves, RF and Blue Light .... Plus, Social Media/ Video Games = instant gratification = and total demolition of my dopamine receptors.

Generation Y also has the highest rate of Suicide, and it is the biggest killer of our Youth today.... _anyone say Dopamine?_

For my generation, having a support network is critical. None of us are retired, and don't have stability that others do. It gives us an advantage as to being "free". But, that ain't really the truth when you haven't got any money or assets behind you. Everything is a leap of faith... and when you have EHS, and you are sick, you seriously cannot afford to fuck up... because of the financial risks it poses. The reality could be you needing Social Support and reliance on the Government for money......... well, that is definitely somewhere you don't want to be.

You need parachute after parachute. And often, you get fed bad advice from parents and peers who just don't understand, **and noone teaches how to think for yourself - and you end up making seriously bad errors that are often costly.**

I've been very lucky to have a Job that pays so well and that allows me to work Freelance. When I left London - I couldn't make it into work more than 3 days a week, and had to have 2 hours extra off in the morning - I had a great job who allowed me to do this - BUT is that the same for everyone?

These days, my Redox is a lot better.... But, I have to do every little thing right to feel alright and make it in.... I can't afford to be wasting 2 months on an Island not knowing why it ain't working and just blaming myself.... I need sensible and sound advice from fellow forum members who will inform me of the risks and pitfalls I could face in my low dopamine/ low redox state - because I can't think well for myself, due to my low redox.

We are all on this forum to learn, and to grow, but it is a community and I believe a lot of people come here looking for People to Pack their Parachutes..... and I'd like to be the guy that gives them every possible angle on what it will be like jumping out of that plane.... because ultimately, their mitochondria will experience it different to everyone else - but being aware of the possibilities shuts down the Dunning Kruger Effect.... You can't truly control a Black Swan Event, or predict it... but you can prepare, and prepare well.

I love this picture Jack put up recently, it really shows what Life is like when we are in tough situations with our Circle of 6.... I think this is happening on the Forum right here and now...........

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

We are all trying to help one another, but we are seeing things from two totally different perspectives.

_"The man doesn't know that there is a snake underneath.
The woman doesn't know that there is a stone crushing the man.
The woman thinks: “I am going to fall! and I can't climb because the snake is going to bite me! Why can't the man use a little more strength and pull me up!"
The man thinks: “I am in so much pain! Yet I'm still pulling you as much as I can! why don't you try and climb a little harder?!" The moral is: You can't see the pressure the other person is under, and the other person can't see the pain that you're in._
**_This is life, no matter whether it's with work, family, feelings or friends we should try to understand each other. MAKE YOUR PERCEPTION SEE ANOTHER'S REALITY. MANY THINK THEY KNOW THE WHOLE STORY BUT RARELY DO._**
_Learn to think differently, perhaps more clearly and communicate better by learning to listen better.
A little thought and patience go a long way."_

I'm glad people feel good on Isla. It sucks to go somewhere so nice and not be able to enjoy it.

Hopefully I don't sound like i'm beating on it - which I'm not. But, I'm trying to present a whole picture of it before everyone just makes it out to be the best place on Earth... cause for me, and others, it didn't do the trick.

"Make your perception see another's reality"............. Context is everything

I've had conversations with people like you describe, like other than just you dude. We didn't have an exclusive relationship did we b/c I'm kinda a convo slut? I love the stories and ideas of most people here.

As much as it's distressing to see mud wrestling here that isn't of the bikini clad women type (Probably not an appropriate Q&A request, right?) . It is useful information to see two people at the elephant adamant on it being a tree trunk vs a wall.

True confession, as I work through my own learning I always hope I'm not the one calling out "No, it's a shoulder deep warm hole that smells funny!"

I didn't have the words earlier for this but it feels like I'm in a lab in the early 1900s hearing:
"Dude! It's a wave!"
"No Bro! It's a particle!"

What if the picture is a little bigger?
