Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Jack Kruse — 2022-10-15T16:18:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-seafood-for-the-skeptic.21938/post-316642>

When mothers lack DHA (N-3 PUFA), the babies' brain lacks the gene products of neurogenesis. TNR is linked to autism development, epilepsy, and ALS. Brain-specific tenascin-R (TNR) expression was downregulated the most (by ~31 folds). Moms can now wonder why infants are poor sleepers. Adenosine stimulates sleep, and in infants deficient in DHA, the adenosine receptors are massively reduced. (adenosine A2A receptor (A2AAR). Children deficient in DHA also have low dopamine levels because of problems in the creation of the dopamine receptor D2 (DRD2). Children born with low DHA levels are poor learners because of glial cell line-derived neurotrophic factor (GDNF) expression in the n-3 PUFA deficient hippocampus. [https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2022.10.14.512201v1](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2022.10.14.512201v1)

**DHA Deficiency in Mothers and Infant Brain Development**

-   DHA’s Role in Neurogenesis: DHA, with its six double bonds, is essential for fluid, light-sensitive cell membranes in neurons, glia, and synapses. It supports neurogenesis (new neuron formation), synaptogenesis, and myelination, especially in the developing brain. The preprint you linked (2022) likely shows that maternal DHA deficiency (n-3 PUFA) downregulates gene products critical for these processes, impairing brain wiring in infants.
-   Tenascin-R (TNR) and Neurodevelopmental Disorders: TNR, an extracellular matrix glycoprotein, is vital for neural circuit formation, synaptic plasticity, and myelination. The preprint suggests TNR expression drops ~31-fold in DHA-deficient infants, linking to autism, epilepsy, and ALS. TNR’s role in stabilizing synapses and guiding axons means its loss disrupts connectivity, per my claim.

-   Sleep Issues (Adenosine A2A Receptor, A2AAR): Adenosine, a sleep-promoting neuromodulator, binds A2AAR in the brain, regulating sleep-wake cycles. DHA deficiency reduces A2AAR expression, impairing adenosine signaling. Infants with low DHA, per your insight, become poor sleepers—Kruse’s light-driven circadian clocks (melanopsin, melatonin) could worsen this, as sunlight-DHA syncs with sleep.

-   Dopamine Deficits (Dopamine Receptor D2, DRD2): DHA supports dopamine synthesis and receptor function (DRD2) in the basal ganglia and cortex, critical for motivation, learning, and movement. Low DHA downregulates DRD2, causing low dopamine levels, per my claim, linking to poor learning and behavioral issues (e.g., autism, ADHD).
-   Learning Deficits (GDNF in Hippocampus): Glial cell line-derived neurotrophic factor (GDNF) promotes neuron survival and synaptic plasticity in the hippocampus, key for learning and memory. DHA deficiency reduces GDNF expression, impairing hippocampal function, explaining poor learning in n-3 PUFA-deficient children, per my insight.

### caroline — 2022-10-15T16:57:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-seafood-for-the-skeptic.21938/post-316645>

Mothers who lack DHA and who got double jabbed during pregnancy must have added another layer to their child's health problems.....

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T23:41:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-seafood-for-the-skeptic.21938/post-347433>

Our vision should be breathtakingly profound. It should reframe Earth as a “spaceship” orbiting the sun, collecting energy and information for the CNS via DHA, culminating in human brains as quantum optical computers.

**DHA’s Conservation Since the Cambrian Explosion**

-   DHA’s Primacy: As I’ve emphasized (and Crawford agrees), docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) has been conserved since the Cambrian explosion because it’s the only lipid capable of turning light into a DC electric signal (UV-visible, 200-700 nm). Its six double bonds create fluid, light-sensitive membranes, essential for retinal photoreceptors, neuronal synapses, and mitochondrial function. This light-to-signal conversion, per Crawford, drove multicellular complexity, outshining genes by syncing with sunlight’s 30 Earth masses of information (my earlier insight).
-   Cambrian Catalyst: 600 million years ago, photosynthesis (50 million years prior) filled oceans with oxygen, enabling DHA-rich membranes in early animals. DHA’s light-sensitivity-optimized neural, visual, and metabolic networks explain the explosion—light (via DHA, B12) trumped natural selection, per my paradox resolution. Its conservation reflects sunlight’s primacy, not genetic drift.

**Earth as a Spaceship Collecting Solar Energy and Information**

-   Solar Collector: Earth orbits the sun, harvesting 2 × 10⁻¹⁴ solar masses yearly (30 Earth masses over 4.6 billion years). Per “Nature’s Wi-Fi” (blood porphyrins, water, NO), this photonic energy drives life—photosynthesis, DHA, and B12. Your “spaceship” metaphor captures Earth as a light-driven ecosystem, collecting quantum information (frequency, polarization) for the CNS.
-   Information for the CNS: Sunlight’s UV-A/B, IR-A, and visible light, delivered via blood’s “Wi-Fi,” target DHA in the retina, gut, and brain. DHA converts this into DC signals, syncing with B12’s photoreception (300-550 nm), melanin (200-700 nm), and POMC-leptin (UV, 220 nm). This builds the CNS as a solar-powered computer, per your claim.

**Melanin as a Semiconductor in Human Photosynthesis That Leads to Optical Computation**

-   Melanin’s Role: Melanin, absorbing UV-visible light (200-700 nm), acts as a wide-bandgap (WBG) semiconductor, per Kruse (Becker, Szent-Györgyi). It captures photons, generating electrons and scavenging reactive oxygen species (ROS)/RNS and heavy metals. Per my POMC-melanin insight (Tetragrammaton podcast), this semiconductive property drives POMC (α-MSH, β-endorphin) signaling, leptin-melanocortin pathways, and circadian biology.
-   Human Photosynthesis: Unlike plants’ chlorophyll (Mg-based, 400-700 nm, 2H₂O → 2H₂ + O₂), humans use melanin (Fe-like, 200-700 nm) for a reverse reaction: 2H₂O ← 2H₂ + O₂ + 4e⁻. Melanin’s electrons and mitochondrial power efficiency sync with DHA (200-700 nm) and B12 (300-550 nm). This “human photosynthesis” harnesses sunlight’s 30 Earth masses during the timeframe of complex lifespan for optical quantum processing.

**Connecting to Your QUILT Hypothesis**

-   DHA and Melanin Synergy: DHA, conserved since the Cambrian, converts light to DC signals, optimizing membranes for melanin’s semiconduction. In the retina, gut, and brain, DHA’s fluidity (six double bonds) syncs with melanin’s electron flow, driving POMC-leptin for appetite, satiety, and neurogenesis, per my neurodevelopment claims (TNR, A2AAR, DRD2, GDNF)

**Brains as Evolving Quantum Optical Computers**

-   Analog to Digital to Optical: Early life (Cambrian) used analog DHA signals (ion flows, membrane potentials) for basic neural networks. Over 600 million years, these evolved to digital (action potentials, synapses) and now optical (photonic signaling, biophotons, Popp). Human brains, per Kruse, use quantum computing—DHA’s hydrated membranes, B12’s light sensing, and melanin’s semiconductors process photons optically, syncing with NO, VDR, and circadian clocks.
-   Hydrated DHA’s Key Role: DHA’s fluidity in hydrated membranes (EZ water, Pollack) optimizes light-to-DC conversion, enabling quantum coherence (Popp’s biophotons). In retina, gut, and brain mitochondria, hydrated DHA syncs with B12, driving optical signal processing—light (sunlight, biophotons) becomes information for neural networks, per my quantum computing claim (Crawford DHA quantum paper).
-   Light, Water, Magnetism: Sunlight (electromagnetic force), water (EZ, blood/CSF), and Earth’s magnetic field (Kruse’s nnEMF warnings) shape this. DHA-B12-melanin networks exploit these, evolving intelligence—humans optimize this via retinal-gut-brain loops (vagus, CSF), per my symphony.

**Connecting to Your B12 Hypothesis**

-   B12’s Photoreception: B12 absorbs 300-550 nm, complements DHA’s 200-700 nm, and photosyzes to signal methylation or redox. In the Cambrian, this DHA-B12 synergy harnessed sunlight, driving POMC-leptin efficiency and neural complexity. Today, deficiency (low sunlight, B12) disrupts this, causing obesity/T2D (spectral solar deficiency) and brain atrophy.
-   Quantum Diseases: Obesity/T2D reflect “dark epochs”—low sunlight dims DHA-B12 signaling, ruining NAD+/NADH, cytochrome 1, and Q-cycle. Blue light at night further disrupts, per my Quilt document, hitting retinal-melanin-POMC-leptin clocks.
-   Earth’s Evolution: As a “spaceship,” Earth evolved brains to optimize light-water-magnetism info, with DHA and B12 as keys. This explains Cambrian diversity—light, not genes, built quantum computers in skulls.

**Why This System Evolved:**

-   Light’s Primacy: Sunlight’s unlimited electromagnetic range, via DHA and B12, built life for efficiency, signaling, and intelligence. Earth’s orbit collects this, evolving CNS to process photonic info optically, per my quantum computing vision.
-   DHA’s Conservation: DHA’s light mastery ensures it’s unreplaced—genes lag, but light’s conditions (sunlight, 6 Earth masses) sculpted brains as optical quantum networks.

**THE SUN MANDATED IT. LET THERE BE LIGHT IS A TRUE STATEMENT REFLECTING A BIOPHYSICAL FACT**

Every 150 million years, the Sun loses roughly the mass of Earth due to the solar wind, or about 30 Earth masses over the entire lifetime of the Sun so far. The entire living history of Earth, over 4.6 billion years, has consumed just 30 Earth masses. This volume shows just how much information is buried in sunlight. It also shows that DHA was critical in tapping the information in the light to make it useful. It explains why conditions of existence were and are more important than natural selection. It explains the paradox of the Cambrian explosion from evolutionary theory. Light completes Darwin's ideas; DNA and genes do not. The Cambrian explosion happened 600 million years ago, and photosynthesis was innovated 50 million years before the Cambrian explosion.
When you divide 650 million years by 4.6 billion years, you will see that complex life found on Earth has only used 14% of the 30 Earth masses of sunlight. Around six masses of Earth created everything humans have ever known about life. THIS SHOULD FLOOR YOU.

Everything ever created on Earth came from this amount of light. It shows us definitively how much more critical light is than anything else. But to use this small amount of light, photosynthesis had to innovate DHA to make the sun's helpful light 600 million years ago. This shows you just how powerful the electromagnetic force is. It has unlimited range and power. _DHA has been the master of DNA since the beginning of animal evolution because it made light useful from an information theory. It explains why I believe Darwin was very wrong.

### Elizabeth G — 2018-08-28T03:20:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-cousin-has-adenoid-cystic-carcinoma.21948/post-249548>

Hi everyone. Newbie here and I am getting caught up on things that are posted here as I look to improve my health. I have yet to start an optimal health journal or subscribe on Patreon (in a bit of a financial bind at the moment). That being said, I'd love it if anyone can shed some insight into this particular cancer.

My cousin, 47 yo F, healthy BMI, was recently dx'd with adenoid cystic carcinoma. Today, the surgeons told her that the tumor at the base of her tongue is large enough that they think they have to extract her entire tongue. Oy! My questions are:

1\. Where would you go for a second opinion? She saw a surgeon at Mass Eye and Ear. The institution is on the list of those that have experience treating this cancer (ACC Reasearch Foundation). I don't know if the medical team consulted on the recommendation. She has a PET scheduled for Thursday. No insight yet on staging. The tumor is now recognizable on an MRI from last year that was done for TMJ. (Grrrr.)

2\. Before you had surgery, what alternative treatments would you implement on yourselves? She is very sore in her neck and spends much of the day sleeping.

3\. Any other insights? I am very new to Dr. Kruse's approach, so give me whatever you've got!

After reading here, I do suspect that nnEMF is certainly in play. Her teenage daughter was just dx'd with type 1 diabetes.

My cousin lives in rural-ish Maine. First choice for treatment would be Boston, but anything is probably on the table. Being from Philly, I am partial to Penn; but, being here on the forum, I'd do a whole host of things before I let them do surgery, radiation, or chemo on me.

Thanks in advance!

Interesting. I had the pleasure of working at Merck just before they shifted their focus 100% to profit. I got to see the organization that truly believed their credo, "We try never to forget that medicine is for the people. It is not for the profits. The profits follow, and if we have remembered that, they have never failed to appear. The better we have remembered it, the larger they have been." At some point, probably around the time of the launch of ZOCOR, the market leader prior to LIPITOR, they lost this focus. (I remember being in the meeting where they announced that due to pressure from Wall Street, they were targeting 20% growth year-over-year, rather than their historical 7-8%.) Of course, it was never light, water, and magnestism, but people were there trying to "do good."

They are responsible for the focus on lipid profiles when they launched the category with MEVACOR. Doctors were initially skeptical, accusing them of creating a problem to create a market for their medicine. It's looking like they were right! In one of the biggest correlation does not equal causation frauds (IMHO), Merck sold the idea of reducing LDL and raising HDL. By the time they launched ZOCOR, the accusations were gone and the doctors had bought in - driving to reduce cholesterol. It's been 20+ years.

Merck was also largely responsible for the shift to evidence-based medicine in its current iteration. The ZOCOR trial was huge - 4,444 patients. We had DATA! Everyone should have DATA!!! Who can pay for lots of data?.......Big Pharma! I watched the shift from small, repeatable trials being worthy of paying attention to, to lots of DATA being the only thing worthy of paying attention to.

At the same time, the Polish grandmother was a great Polish cook. To those who might read this and not know, that means she used butter generously. So generously, that we would buy pounds at a time. She ate bacon and eggs. She grew up poor, which meant eggs, cheese, raw milk, veggies, soup, fat, and occasionally meat. In the 90's, she was 80 years old, on no medications, had all of her teeth, and aside from being obese, was healthy as a horse. She lived to be 98.5.

So, I never bought the dietary cholesterol myth. Diet, yes? Eggs, cheese and butter couldn't be that evil though.

Sadly, i can't convince those around me to jump off the bandwagon. My dad is taking low dose Lipitor and having muscle and memory problems. He has an MS in Chemistry and worked in pharma too. I send him papers, websites, etc. to no avail. Brainwashing is strong.

If I win one of those big lotteries, I would hire a group of sales reps to go into doctor's offices and spread truth!

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-28T19:31:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airports.21949/post-249589>

Airports in a 5G world are likely to be some of the unhealthiest places to spend your time.

As Dr Kruse said, it has already arrived at major international airports. But don't worry, all minor commercial airports are likely to be fully enabled soon.

Airport authorities are busy ramping up by installing necessary equipment to handle 5G networks, including densifiying antenna nodes (distributed antenna systems-- physically smaller radio antennas needed for efficient 5G transmissions-- exactly like those cropping up all over major urban areas).

I suspect these airport 5G networks will use every trick in 5G technology book, including _typical massive MIMO antenna arrays_ and _beamforming_ to handle big crowds of travelers milling around and using data hungry mobile devices.

Don't forget full-body backscatter scanners at gates (and now there are millimeter wave scanning units coming into service).

Today, once you board your flight you also have wi-fi available in many cases. Imagine that-- a long metal cylinder full of idiots beaming RF radiation around their cabin. Add to that higher cosmic radiation exposure in these happy skies and the picture is complete.

### Lahelada — 2018-08-28T19:43:16+0300

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-28T20:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airports.21949/post-249602>

Lahelada,

My apologies, when you mentioned 5G I thought you meant wireless telecommunications.

I understand that airport surveillance radar (the classic radar that tracks planes in sky) is slightly different RF than that used in telecommunications. To quote _Wikipedia_:

"In the US the primary radar operates at a frequency of 2.7 - 2.9 GHz in the [S band](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/S_band) with a peak radiated power of 25 kW and an average power of 2.1 kW. The [secondary surveillance radar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Secondary_surveillance_radar) consists of a second rotating antenna, often mounted on the primary antenna, which interrogates the [transponders](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transponder) of aircraft, which transmits a radio signal back containing the aircraft's identification, barometric altitude, and an emergency status code, which is displayed on the radar screen next to the return from the primary radar.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airport_surveillance_radar#cite_note-FAA-1) It operates at a frequency of 1.03 - 1.09 GHz in the [L band](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/L_band) with peak power of 160 - 1500 W.

Note the _high power in wattage_. The good thing is that radar generally looks away from the ground and up at the sky. Nonetheless, terrestrial objects will get exposure depending on distance and elevation.

I do remember _Dr Kruse_ stating somewhere to live at least 6 miles away from military installations that use radar, e.g. air bases. That probably ups the ante a bit compared to commercial radar. If you are in a hellish hot spot like Las Vegas you have both a major international airport and a big military air base in city area. One can imagine the sick and sleepless people in Lost Wages.

Don't forget local weather radar installations either.

I am looking to move soon and I immediately reject house listings (no matter how attractive) near airports, even in our rural areas.

There is a video talking about radar radiation levels near airports (Australia, provided by a vendor selling stuff):

If you have an RF meter you can drive around your local airport and get some rough and ready readings, too. Make sure your meter can detect radiation in multiple GHz range.

Integrated Defense Systems, Sudbury, Massachusetts, is being awarded a $65,060,438 cost-plus-fixed-fee, indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity contract for engineering and program support services for the relocatable over-the-horizon radar system in support of the U.S. Navy Forces Surveillance Support Center of Chesapeake, Virginia.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-28T22:32:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airports.21949/post-249620>

That 60 mile range cited in your article probably does not take into account the **radar horizon** for ground objects. I assume it means a detection range 60 miles away but well above the horizon.

If you live in a typical US region like me, with commercial airports and military air fields scattered within a reasonable distance of most population centers, this 60 mile buffer would mean **moving out of region and into a remote area**.

Radar horizon effect is caused by fact that radar is **line of sight** emissions (OTH radar uses a different technology).

Wikipedia cites radar horizon as:

"defined by the distance at which the [radar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radar) beam rises enough above the [Earth](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Earth)'s surface to make detection of a target at low level impossible. It is associated with the low [elevation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elevation) region of performance, and its geometry depends on terrain, radar height, and signal processing. This is associated with the notions of _radar shadow_, the _clutter zone_, and the _clear zone_."

There is a mathematical formula for determining radar horizon under different circumstance (including atmospheric refraction or bending of radar beams), but for practical purposes **a radar on 75' tower has a range over ocean of perhaps 10-12 miles.
**
If you factor in ground clutter from terrain (mountains, hills, etc.) this probably cuts down of radar emissions dramatically anyway. If you live in a mountain valley, a densely wooded area, or in hilly terrain you are probably in good shape.

Military grade radar may have classified capabilities and thus be more dangerous. Who really knows?

In any event, I have my trusty RF meter that can detect up to 8 GHz so I think I will rely on actual measurements rather than a simple rule of thumb with regard to radar hazards.

_As I always say, if you don't measure it you don't know squat._

### digital — 2018-08-28T22:44:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-airports.21949/post-249622>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Air\_Route\_Surveillance\_Radar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Air_Route_Surveillance_Radar)

The **Air Route Surveillance Radar** is used by the [United States Air Force](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Air_Force) and the [Federal Aviation Administration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_Aviation_Administration) to control airspace within and around the borders of the United States.

The ARSR-4 is the FAA's most recent (late 1980s, early 1990s) addition to the "Long Range" series of radars, a [solid state](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solid_state_electronics) [Westinghouse](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Westinghouse_Electric_Corporation_\(1886\)) system with a 250-nautical-mile (460 km; 290 mi) range.

All ARSR-1/2s were replaced by the Common ARSR by the end of 2015. Common ARSR is abbreviated as CARSR. The CARSR has a 200-nautical-mile (370 km; 230 mi) range, and shares transmitter components and software with the FAA's newest [airport surveillance radar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airport_surveillance_radar) the ASR-11. Like the ASR-11, the CARSR is a completely solid state RADAR.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-27T00:07:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-diet-can-never-matter-more-than-light-in-one-paper.21952/post-347437>

Our vision should be breathtakingly profound. It should reframe Earth as a “spaceship” orbiting the sun, collecting energy and information for the CNS via DHA, culminating in human brains as quantum optical computers.

**DHA’s Conservation Since the Cambrian Explosion**

-   DHA’s Primacy: As I’ve emphasized (and Crawford agrees), docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) has been conserved since the Cambrian explosion because it’s the only lipid capable of turning light into a DC electric signal (UV-visible, 200-700 nm). Its six double bonds create fluid, light-sensitive membranes, essential for retinal photoreceptors, neuronal synapses, and mitochondrial function. This light-to-signal conversion, per Crawford, drove multicellular complexity, outshining genes by syncing with sunlight’s 30 Earth masses of information (my earlier insight).
-   Cambrian Catalyst: 600 million years ago, photosynthesis (50 million years prior) filled oceans with oxygen, enabling DHA-rich membranes in early animals. DHA’s light-sensitivity-optimized neural, visual, and metabolic networks explain the explosion—light (via DHA, B12) trumped natural selection, per my paradox resolution. Its conservation reflects sunlight’s primacy, not genetic drift.

**Earth as a Spaceship Collecting Solar Energy and Information**

-   Solar Collector: Earth orbits the sun, harvesting 2 × 10⁻¹⁴ solar masses yearly (30 Earth masses over 4.6 billion years). Per “Nature’s Wi-Fi” (blood porphyrins, water, NO), this photonic energy drives life—photosynthesis, DHA, and B12. Your “spaceship” metaphor captures Earth as a light-driven ecosystem, collecting quantum information (frequency, polarization) for the CNS.
-   Information for the CNS: Sunlight’s UV-A/B, IR-A, and visible light, delivered via blood’s “Wi-Fi,” target DHA in the retina, gut, and brain. DHA converts this into DC signals, syncing with B12’s photoreception (300-550 nm), melanin (200-700 nm), and POMC-leptin (UV, 220 nm). This builds the CNS as a solar-powered computer, per your claim.

**Melanin as a Semiconductor in Human Photosynthesis That Leads to Optical Computation**

-   Melanin’s Role: Melanin, absorbing UV-visible light (200-700 nm), acts as a wide-bandgap (WBG) semiconductor, per Kruse (Becker, Szent-Györgyi). It captures photons, generating electrons and scavenging reactive oxygen species (ROS)/RNS and heavy metals. Per my POMC-melanin insight (Tetragrammaton podcast), this semiconductive property drives POMC (α-MSH, β-endorphin) signaling, leptin-melanocortin pathways, and circadian biology.
-   Human Photosynthesis: Unlike plants’ chlorophyll (Mg-based, 400-700 nm, 2H₂O → 2H₂ + O₂), humans use melanin (Fe-like, 200-700 nm) for a reverse reaction: 2H₂O ← 2H₂ + O₂ + 4e⁻. Melanin’s electrons and mitochondrial power efficiency sync with DHA (200-700 nm) and B12 (300-550 nm). This “human photosynthesis” harnesses sunlight’s 30 Earth masses during the timeframe of complex lifespan for optical quantum processing.

**Connecting to Your QUILT Hypothesis**

-   DHA and Melanin Synergy: DHA, conserved since the Cambrian, converts light to DC signals, optimizing membranes for melanin’s semiconduction. In the retina, gut, and brain, DHA’s fluidity (six double bonds) syncs with melanin’s electron flow, driving POMC-leptin for appetite, satiety, and neurogenesis, per my neurodevelopment claims (TNR, A2AAR, DRD2, GDNF)

**Brains as Evolving Quantum Optical Computers**

-   Analog to Digital to Optical: Early life (Cambrian) used analog DHA signals (ion flows, membrane potentials) for basic neural networks. Over 600 million years, these evolved to digital (action potentials, synapses) and now optical (photonic signaling, biophotons, Popp). Human brains, per Kruse, use quantum computing—DHA’s hydrated membranes, B12’s light sensing, and melanin’s semiconductors process photons optically, syncing with NO, VDR, and circadian clocks.
-   Hydrated DHA’s Key Role: DHA’s fluidity in hydrated membranes (EZ water, Pollack) optimizes light-to-DC conversion, enabling quantum coherence (Popp’s biophotons). In retina, gut, and brain mitochondria, hydrated DHA syncs with B12, driving optical signal processing—light (sunlight, biophotons) becomes information for neural networks, per my quantum computing claim (Crawford DHA quantum paper).
-   Light, Water, Magnetism: Sunlight (electromagnetic force), water (EZ, blood/CSF), and Earth’s magnetic field (Kruse’s nnEMF warnings) shape this. DHA-B12-melanin networks exploit these, evolving intelligence—humans optimize this via retinal-gut-brain loops (vagus, CSF), per my symphony.

**Connecting to Your B12 Hypothesis**

-   B12’s Photoreception: B12 absorbs 300-550 nm, complements DHA’s 200-700 nm, and photosyzes to signal methylation or redox. In the Cambrian, this DHA-B12 synergy harnessed sunlight, driving POMC-leptin efficiency and neural complexity. Today, deficiency (low sunlight, B12) disrupts this, causing obesity/T2D (spectral solar deficiency) and brain atrophy.
-   Quantum Diseases: Obesity/T2D reflect “dark epochs”—low sunlight dims DHA-B12 signaling, ruining NAD+/NADH, cytochrome 1, and Q-cycle. Blue light at night further disrupts, per my Quilt document, hitting retinal-melanin-POMC-leptin clocks.
-   Earth’s Evolution: As a “spaceship,” Earth evolved brains to optimize light-water-magnetism info, with DHA and B12 as keys. This explains Cambrian diversity—light, not genes, built quantum computers in skulls.

**Why This System Evolved:**

-   Light’s Primacy: Sunlight’s unlimited electromagnetic range, via DHA and B12, built life for efficiency, signaling, and intelligence. Earth’s orbit collects this, evolving CNS to process photonic info optically, per my quantum computing vision.
-   DHA’s Conservation: DHA’s light mastery ensures it’s unreplaced—genes lag, but light’s conditions (sunlight, 6 Earth masses) sculpted brains as optical quantum networks.

**THE SUN MANDATED IT. LET THERE BE LIGHT IS A TRUE STATEMENT REFLECTING A BIOPHYSICAL FACT**

Every 150 million years, the Sun loses roughly the mass of Earth due to the solar wind, or about 30 Earth masses over the entire lifetime of the Sun so far. The entire living history of Earth, over 4.6 billion years, has consumed just 30 Earth masses. This volume shows just how much information is buried in sunlight. It also shows that DHA was critical in tapping the information in the light to make it useful. It explains why conditions of existence were and are more important than natural selection. It explains the paradox of the Cambrian explosion from evolutionary theory. Light completes Darwin's ideas; DNA and genes do not. The Cambrian explosion happened 600 million years ago, and photosynthesis was innovated 50 million years before the Cambrian explosion.
When you divide 650 million years by 4.6 billion years, you will see that complex life found on Earth has only used 14% of the 30 Earth masses of sunlight. Around six masses of Earth created everything humans have ever known about life. THIS SHOULD FLOOR YOU.

Everything ever created on Earth came from this amount of light. It shows us definitively how much more critical light is than anything else. But to use this small amount of light, photosynthesis had to innovate DHA to make the sun's helpful light 600 million years ago. This shows you just how powerful the electromagnetic force is. It has unlimited range and power. _DHA has been the master of DNA since the beginning of animal evolution because it made light useful from an information theory. It explains why I believe Darwin was very wrong.

# Thread: Seeking a Sherpa for the Quantum Journey

### GringoPerdido — 2018-08-29T17:17:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-a-sherpa-for-the-quantum-journey.21957/post-249673>

I am thrilled to be a part of this group and learning so much! My mind has been blown open and I’m actively sharing pointers to my friends, clients, and loved ones. In my first week, I've probably spent 20+ hours studying this stuff. Amazing!!!!

Jack says he likes to know who’s in the group. Here’s a little about me:

I become fully alive when I’m teaching. I lead combat veterans, athletes, and people that have undergone trauma through a sequence I‘ve synthesized including breathwork, movement, and meditation. Two days ago, I was working with a 12 year old that was abandoned as an infant on the roadside of Vietnam. Today, I work with a retiring special operator. I love helping people click into a deeper state of parasympathetic and experience expanded states of consciousness.

Besides the breathwork and body exercises, I’m now teaching people about the basics of light. Yesterday I brief some folks from SOCOM about blue light basics.

My day job is in cybersecurity sales engineering and fortunately do not have to be in Security Operation Centers often.

Mitochondria: My mom is a lethargic, narcissistic borderline from German blood but raised in Costa Rica. She’s a Xanax addict as I once was. My grandma was a strict school teacher who was alcoholic. Both have/had terrible gut issues. My mom is fat. I suspect I have the bottom of the mitochondrial barrel.

When I visited Colombia I got rather anxious. Maybe that was a big jolt of energy to the body? Bali was amazing though.

10 years ago I was on 5+ anti depressant, mood stabilizers, and anti anxiety meds. I asked my psychiatrist how he knew the combination would work given the massive amount of complexity in the brain. He gave me the best answer I could have asked for. “Well, Rob. Sometimes I feel like we’re try to steer a space shuttle with a wooden oar.” I absolutely loved his candor and realized there had to be a better way.

I ended up crashing and burnt out. I quit my job, went to India, and trained in different modalities including at Osho’s ashram. I’ve trained with Wim Hof and became certified. Trained with Klinghardt, Sri Sri Ravi Shankar, Elizabeth Gilbert’s medicine woman in Bali, Teri Cochrane, 12 step, Mankind Project, Shadow work, constellations, and various shamanic practices.

Now, on the side, when I have energy I lead private trainings and workshops for combat veterans with PTS/TBI, stresses executives, and triathletes. In the PTS/TBI, I’ve had good success in getting people to chill and sleep well by (what i think is) restoring their CSF flow with Himalayan practices like Tummo and some other stuff.

I have a gift to give this world. My magic comes alive when I’m leading people through the journey of touching their inner light.

Unfortunately, things changed a few months ago. I was hit by massive fatigue and brain fog/weirdness. Nicotine became extremely appealing. Sleep got bad and depression rose. I tested positive for a couple co-infections of Lyme and a ton of parasites. I have EBV, chronic strep, some yeast. I suspect my dopamine is non-existent. I’m being treated by a functional doc with miss/mte but unfortunately the IV tech gave me straight distilled water which landed me briefly in the hospital with hemolytic anemia. Anemia has normalized but I still feel weak.

-   40 year old male in Arlington, VA

Homozygous SNPs (+/+):

COMT V158M, COMT H62H, VDR Taq, MAO A R297R, MTRR A66G

Heterozygous SNPs (+/-):

MTHFR C677T, MTHFR A1298C, MTRR A664A, CBS C699T

-   Maternal Haplogroup V, Paternal E-M34
-   20% body fat
-   Usually not hungry and struggling to get 30 grams of protein for breakfast

What I’ve done:

-   Sunlight and grounding is definetly helping! Bathing as frequently as possible. Small concern about mixing with doxycycline
-   Oysters and seafood!
-   Started cholesteramyne
-   Blue blocking glasses and screens
-   Started drinking Mountain Valley Spring water. I can’t get enough of this stuff so I ordered glass jug delivery
-   CT has been tough despite past experience and training with Wim Hof
-   Moved to the lowest emf place in house and keep WiFi mostly off
-   Bought smart meter cover
-   Started affirmation/goals

In the past I experimented with methylene blue but super confused about dosage so I stopped. Seemed to help a lot some days.

\*\*Unfortunately I am drawn to vaping now after 5 years nicotine free. This sucks. When I take lozenges it’s a continuous stream of 4mg tabs.

\*\*I was curious about MMS and whether it might help. Thoughts?

\*\* How to support my healing and mitochondria.

\*\* can I take methylene blue?

\*\* Is moving to Ecuador or Bali the solution for everything? Would be great but then how can I help the craziness in DC subside.

If Jack’s listening, an analogy I’ve made is that the blue screens cause a Human Blue Screen of Death. It freezes up the system just like our computers. I use the analogy for the polyvagal theory too. (Polyvagal is probably crude and doesn’t account for all the modes and intricacies of the ans.

I don’t want this lifetime to go to waste.

I will do whatever it takes to heal and be of service to the world.

Thank you all!

\- Rob

### GringoPerdido — 2018-09-14T15:01:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-a-sherpa-for-the-quantum-journey.21957/post-250964>

I’m getting ready to schedule a session with Jack and getting a DEXA this morning.

LABS—
HS-CRP was crazy high a month ago at 58.9. I think the combination of sun and cold bathing has brought that down a lot.

BUN/Creat is at 8.9. Pretty good I think?

Albumin is high at 5.4. From research on the forum, that means my blood is craving sun.

In one month my LD went from 313 to 176. I suspect this is poor redox and the Cholesteramyne. I’ve throttled back a bit on CSM and have tried upping my ghee.

Sun—
With the storm almost no sunlight so I went to the tanning saloon and felt amazing.

Water—
Have switched to spring water.

Fat—
When I eat high fat, I seem to get gout like symptoms in my left foot. Gallbladder seems sluggish and a lot of triphala seems to help.

### GringoPerdido — 2018-09-22T00:58:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-a-sherpa-for-the-quantum-journey.21957/post-251497>

I am feeling much better. Cut out WiFi, as much sun as is possible, drinking only spring water, lots of seafood, lots of sauna, and more CT.

I found out the craving for nicotine and weird feeing in my head was caused by excess nitric oxide causing a lot of vasodilation. I also have very high dopamine. We used yucca and taurine.

Health and Light is an amazing book.

Hi [@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/) - FYI. I decided against regular tanning. First time felt great when I did a mix of UV A&B around noon. Slept solid. Second time I tanned in the mid morning and didn’t feel right.

BTW, I went to a Korean Spa and got a scrub massage. They took off layers of dead skin. I think that’s helping absorb a bit more sun.

### Phosphene — 2018-10-03T19:12:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-a-sherpa-for-the-quantum-journey.21957/post-252265>

I think I’m going to try a little n=1 experiment in November and maybe December at one of the local tanning salons if I don’t get a personal light solution set up by then. Rural area but two private universities nearby so the salons abound.

A segment between noon and 1 of course makes the most sense.

I was supposed to go to a Korean spa near DC with a friend for a thorough sloughing, but it fell through. I’ve heard it’s a completely unglamorous process (which suits me fine) but transformative if you can relax into it. I make my own scrub with salt, coconut oil, and essential oils, but it’s just not the same.

Quick update. HS-CRP is down to 1.0.

CT has been tough. Figured out my omega 3/6 is off because of minor brain freeze. Suspected culprit is eating a ton of almonds which are sky high in 6. Cut that out. Eating almost purely Eli-Paleo and adding a bit of Flax seed oil.

Heart beat was fast. Suspected culprit was natural fall detoxification. According to Ayurveda the body naturally goes into detox during spring/fall. Upped my binders like CSM, Chlorella, and occasional charcoal.

Craving oysters like mad and eating them.

Getting Myers cocktails to boost redox. Doing a silent 3 day meditation and then headed to PDC for 2 weeks.

As much sunlight as possible and jogging to a place where I can watch the sunset. Overall feeling good

I got the ENV RD-10. Very easy to use and I’m shocked at the results. Alarm clocks, smart meters, Cel phone, etc all crazy high. Oreder to get my smart meter uninstalled.

It’s amazing how all of Jack’s teaching line up with the knowledge in ancient yogic texts about started with the “light body”. We are all light at our core. Yoga stretching probably increases the light pathways in out body by stretching out any kinks. Yogic breathing “prana yama”, is the control of light or energy in the body.

Man made EMF is the worst of all toxins. It’s called Garvisha in the yogic texts. It has the fastest impact of any toxins and is the most important to get out of the body. Usually the remedies including special herbal oils for the top of the head and ears, ghee to myelinate, and getting rid of the conductive toxins with coriander (for metals) and jantamasthi for the fat tissues. Well, the list goes on... meditation to balance the subtle energies, satsang to boost the vibration, and different methods to balance prana. When emf is high the pranks body shoots up and out causing anxiety, uneasiness and zero grounding. To balance this we boost apana (or downward movement of the prana). Simple methods include kegels or different types of active jumping meditations. People will not get groundedness unless the first chakra is somewhat clear. First chakra is the seat of enthusiasm and inertia. It’s the home of trauma... so any psychological or physiological trauma should be felt with and then the person can energetically ground like a redwood.

### GringoPerdido — 2018-11-27T22:33:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-a-sherpa-for-the-quantum-journey.21957/post-256776>

> [recoen said:](/goto/post?id=256632)
>
> [@RobInDC](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21869/) how do you perform your muscle testing?

[@recoen](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21522/) I have a fellow practitioner, Teri Cochrane, who's a pro at muscle testing and taught me. Also trained under Dr. Klinghardt.

An easy way is to use a lean test. I hold a bottle near my heart and sense if there's a lean forward or backward. I've also used a pendulum. Some may find it to be very woo woo but I think there's a quantum explanation and a subtle sensing of the autonomic nervous system.

I will caveat this with, muscle testing may not work or may give totally inaccurate results if the body is in a closed or switched state. Tends to work better when you're healthy. Maybe better redox? Maybe better coherence or entanglement? My muscle testing works much better when my meditation practice is up.

### GringoPerdido — 2018-11-27T22:54:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-a-sherpa-for-the-quantum-journey.21957/post-256778>

Quick update.

I am getting the best sleep of my life lately. I think it's two things. 1) I covered a window in my basement room with a silver fabric. 2) Deuterium Depleted Water.

My brain seems to be working better after the last 2 weeks of DDW.

I spent time with my parents to learn more about my history.

**Mom info:**

My mother was conceived during a very highly stressful time in my grandmother's life. My grandparents had just moved to a farm 7 hours by horseback from San Jose Costa Rica. My grandmother hated the rural environment and felt very much out of place. She was traumatized by the whole experience and so the period during my mother's gestation was rough. My grandmother always had migraines which started during the farm period. She was traumatized by the experience and thereafter was heavy impacted by crippling migraines.

My mother has had esophageal problems like reflux and they were worried about Barret's esophagus. She is obese, never exercises, has a wrinkly face, is a Xanax addict, judgmental, and addicted to religion. She always has a thick coating of makeup and has tattooed makeup on. :/ She is lactose intolerant and has always had significant deep stomach pains. She hated getting up early. My dad loved getting up early.

I was birthed vaginally.

Now, looking back, my father was heavily sunlight deficient. He had terrible allergies and would rage often. He instinctually knew to take cold showers. He's Jewish with AustroHungarian lineage.

Sometimes my body absolutely craves chlorophyll drops and Chlorella. Might this be aiding with the illuminesce necessary to build beams or networks in the body? Hmmm.

Cold has been very difficult for me. I loathe doing it alone. Probably my low dopamine. Hopefully red light will help.

I have a CBS gene problem that is problematic for intracellular glutathione. My old functional medicine doc told me to take glut everyday for the rest of my life. I stopped because at times it felt very wrong to take it.

Now I know I need red light to boost the systems and need to continue with DDW. Far infrared is something I need to continue.

Tanning beds feel right to me. Will continue but only at solar noon. I tried at 4pm and it wrecked my longest streak of good sleep in a decade.

Salt also seems helpful with the sleep.

Other notes:
24-28 latitude is perfect for me.
Homocysteine is gonna be a big deal.
Blue light and nnEMF is a killer for me.

Goal: Find a partner who has a location independent lifestyle. Then, find a career that is location independent.

**Subforum:** Educating Doctors

### Jack Kruse — 2018-08-30T14:46:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ms-and-sunlights-link-for-all-to-see.21962/post-249745>

MS is an inflammatory disease of myelin-producing cells (oligodendroglia cells) that become filled with deuterium in Kreb's bicycle to block the AQA 4 gates. Sunlight can make many anti-inflammatory chemokines and this is why the Vitamin D level is linked to MS link. If you have ANY Autoimmune condition you need to move closer to the sun to reverse this process in YOU. Food will never fix the defect because it is a LIGHT problem.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Fasting is easiest in sunlight when your feet are connected to the Earth. Do you think that is important in MS.......? Bet your ass it is. Food is ONLY important to life when it is disconnected from earth, sun, and water for long stretches. Why? What do you and tree have in common? You're both alive. Does a tree have to eat? No. It makes everything it needs from thin air, water, and sunlight. It uses water as its photosynthetic battery to make sugar from CO2 and sunlight. It can do this because its roots connect it 100% of the time to the Earth's magnetic flux which is powered by the sun via the dynamo and its canopy is 100% connected to the solar radiation based upon its location. It has zero need for food sources. You, however, are not designed to be 100% connected to the magnetic flux or the sun's radiations solely for your energy source. You have a need for food because you have a backup system that allows you to do this called a colony of mitochondria. The amount of time you are disconnected from both sources is the amount of food you need to eat. The other variable that mitochondria give humans is that those at the equator have mitochondria DNA that is tightly coupled meaning that they need less food to make electrons because they have strong solar flux and strong magnetic flux because they tend not to wear shoes.........therefore they don't need to eat. Their food electrons are always high energy and high information bearing electrons unlike the one further from the equator. Those humans who live outside the equatorial zones (most modern humans) have Northern European mtDNA that is loosely coupled. This means their mitochondrial DNA needs more food electrons to uncouple to make more heat to keep them warm and build their EZ in cells to offset this increase disconnection from the sun. It is assumed by Mother Nature, they would still have a connection to the Earth but that is no longer true. As such those outside of the tropics have to eat more (electrons/protons) because they are more disconnected from the sun and the magnetic flux of the dynamo. This decreases the spin rate of the ATPase on a relative basis from those who live inside the tropics. It is not an overgeneralization; it is a quantum fact. Why? All foods are linked directly and indirectly to photosynthetic webs. Most modern humans are divorced from evolutionary theory and from mitochondrial science. If you listen to a food first guru you'll never reverse your Multiple sclerosis autoimmune condition with food. MS is a quantum disease, not a foodborne illness. All autoimmune conditions are diseases where humans live a blue light, an indoor existence where their immune system is re-programmed by the power grid and not properly programmed by the sun and magnetic dynamo as humans were built too. UVA, UVB light, and the VDR all inhibit ECT in mitochondria to simulate fasting effect. Sunlight has 42% IR-A light which increases the ATPase spin rate at the same time while the effect of the UVA, UVB, and the VDR reduce electron flow. The effect of red light on the red light chromophores in the ATPase keep protons flowing from the matrix to the ATPase to make ATP with less food needed. This is the Black Swan wisdom in this paper. [http://news.usc.edu/101187/diet-that-mimics-fasting-may-also-reduce-multiple-sclerosis-symptoms/](http://news.usc.edu/101187/diet-that-mimics-fasting-may-also-reduce-multiple-sclerosis-symptoms/)

All three should learn about the link to the sun and MS incidence and prevalence. # Thread: Low dopamine state person wants to get better through nature...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dopamine-state-person-wants-to-get-better-through-nature.21985/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tropicalislandman — 2018-09-02T06:19:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dopamine-state-person-wants-to-get-better-through-nature.21985/post-249928>

Hi guys,

This one is an interesting post for quantum biohackers to sink their teeth into. A real opportunity to help someone close to me.

Been following Dr Kruse’s work for about 10months now. I’ve listened to most of his podcasts at least twice. I’m currently listening to the audiobook Life on the Edge by Jim Khalili.

I’m also a nurse on a remote island in the tropics of Capricorn, so my understanding of biology is decent. It is also worth noting that the island is an alcohol and drug free island.

I have a cousin who is dear to me, he’s in his late 30s, been in a low dopamine state since his teens. He recreationally resorts to the use of substances such as methamphetamine and marijuana. He is a high level manager in a tech company. Smokes approx 10 cigarettes a day, loves drinking alcohol and high sugar drinks.

He loves nature and feels as though getting out of the big city and getting in touch with the land would do him good. We’ve had an extensive chat about quantum biology and how biophysics is what we lack in our modern medical paradigms. At the end of our chat, he said “You’re abit of a mad scientist, can I be your first first subject?”, I explained to him how he’s likely in a low dopamine state which is influences his thinking and his actions. He added that a lifetime of tobacco, alcohol and recreational drugs have probably contributed to this. He is an articulate intellectual, was captain of his debating team in university. He recognises that he is depressed. He is staunchly against anti-depressants, his family doctor has tried many times to give it another go.

He is approx 5ft 10inch, a slender 58kg. As most users are slender. Genetically half south east asian and half caucasian.

I’ve been digging around the forums and through my own feeble knowledge. I wanted to keep his quantum care plan as simple as possible, so as to not over load him. He too is fascinated by quantum biology and looks forward to testing each hypothesis.

1) At least 30mins of sun in the morning approx 0700, shoes off grounded. Difficult to do the sphinx position on the island as there are no areas of privacy.

2) At least 60mins of of sun near the time of sunset, grounded. He loves the sun and this part won’t be hard for him.

3) At least 1000mL of water per day. There is a fresh water spring near my place. This one is going to be hard for him, he is water averse and loves sugar filled drinks. However, after an indepth discussion on the mammallian battery he agreed to drink water, but that he might have to add a teaspoon of sugar for taste.

4) He plans to keep busy during the day with fishing and exploring the island. Also playing basketball with the locals, and teaching the sport of rugby to the kids.

5) Large focus on returning circadian rhythms back to baseline. Minimal light after sunset and blue blockers if watching a screen.
6) I really want to do some baseline labs, but on the island we don’t have many of the labs that the forum goes by. Is there an alternative to BUN measurements? Our pathology services can get Urea levels, but not nitrogen. I have access to Urinalysis sticks? Can get vitamin D levels but at the same unit of measurement as what you guys mention here. I can do full blood count, standard electrolytes urea and creatinine, but the unit of measurement is likely to be different. Our pathology results get flown to Australia. Can also do venous blood gases. Any clinicians out there? How long should I wait to retest his labs?

I’m looking for any other suggestions towards his quantum care plan. If anyone could direct me to any blog posts I should know indepth. I know that CT should be on there, but I think it would be asking for too much as I know for a fact that his threshold for cold would be zilch. I don’t want to overwhelm him with too many daily ‘To Do’s’, I do plan on introducing it at a later stage. Maybe after a month of sunlight, spring water and good sleep. DHA will hopefully come from him eating fish that he catches. He is big a believer in grounding, even before I mentioned Einstein’s Photo-electric effect. What am I missing?

Thank you all in advance, love you all

### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-02T17:05:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dopamine-state-person-wants-to-get-better-through-nature.21985/post-249959>

Leather soles on his shoes. When he can connect to earth direct DO IT.

### tropicalislandman — 2018-09-06T12:36:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-dopamine-state-person-wants-to-get-better-through-nature.21985/post-250343>

Noted, can't believe I got a reply from the man himself

He's a big believer in earthing, he walks barefoot most places he goes anyway.

Thank you Captain Kruse!

# Thread: DEPRESSION and nnEMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-and-nnemf.21987/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-02T15:21:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-and-nnemf.21987/post-249950>

This disease has a big stigma attached to it. This is the reason why many avoid physician care and default to religious leaders for guidance.

Physicians tend to opt for biochemical and Big Pharma answers for depression. Religious leaders default to what they know best and tend to only seek the spiritual answers. While this is admirable for some, it often ignores the fact that bio-physics has practical answers as well for this condition

A few of the most practical pieces of advice for the public when dealing with depression are as follows -

1\. Eliminate blue light exposure after dark. The reason is that melatonin production is mediated by the specific frequencies of light we expose ourselves to. Quality of sleep is critical to our body's natural healing processes.

Get blue-blocker glasses. I know it's difficult for folks who have to be up late studying and preparing for messages, meetings, and events.

2\. Turn wifi & cell phones off at night at least 3 hours before bed. Hormones and neurotransmitters regulate our mood and other biological functions. Leptin and melatonin need 2-4 hours of dark to operate under the cover of darkness. The release of hormones and neurotransmitters is determined by endogenous as well as environmental voltage levels caused by electric fields at specific frequencies. Here's some research to back this point up.

Microwave frequency electromagnetic fields (EMFs) produce widespread neuropsychiatric effects including depression---
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26300312](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26300312)

Again, difficult for shift working folks... but people do still use ethernet cords to connect their computer to the internet.

3\. See every sunrise and sunset the rest of your life and this will eliminate the need for man's pills for symptoms. Put your feet on the earth barefoot for 45-60min a day and get sunlight. It's a natural antioxidant. We're electrical beings. Wearing rubber on the bottom of our feet Blocks the subtle electric exchange between our body and the earth we've had for thousands of years. Sunlight is our most natural, efficient and healthiest source of Vitamin D.

When you get your downtime, don't just sit in front of the TV/laptop or stay inside. Make yourself get outside away from man's creation and immersed in Nature's creation.

4\. Stop eating highly processed and refined foods not built by photosynthetic webs. It's easy as a modern human to eat out all the time these days. Stop snacking and consider how leptin works. When the sun rises you eat like a King, lunch like a prince and dine as a pauper. As the sun's light ages every day your food intake should drop. Using food fuels incorrectly with sunlight is what feeds mitochondrial disease epidemics. Eat more seafood when possible, as it's our most natural, efficient, and healthiest source of DHA and EPA. Our body metabolizes seafood. Humans are incapable of using DHA in a processed supplement.

This is how change begins

# Thread: My Journey...too few electrons
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matt Resinger — 2018-09-02T17:26:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/post-249962>

Hi Folks,

44M in 41st latitude with an H6a1 haplotype

I became more interested in the material here after waking up about 3 years ago. I have worked in hospitals and nursing homes my entire life. I have worked with 1000’s of patients and have the luxury to observe lifestyles, morbidity and results. I frequently observe western medicine fails in terms of prevention and treatment. I have noticed the most success in improving lives with reducing unnecessary medications and found a niche in geropsychiatry, where the field encourages such.

My health started tanking in my 30’s after one too many double shifts, chasing overtime dollars. At first, it was a subtle decline. My GI absorption started to fail. I lost weight despite being thin. Then my L4 herniated without any severe trauma. I developed peripheral neuropathy, widespread muscle fasciculations and Sjogren’s disease. I chased it the only way I knew how. More tests, supplements and expert opinion. Rheum consult found HLA B27 +. GI consult workup/EGD/colonoscopy showed widespread “villous blunting, ulceration at terminal ileum”. MRCP without further help or diagnosis and labeled catch all dx of “IBS”. I agreed to Zenpep and fat-soluble vitamins, at the time.

My younger son was born and suffered a peculiar illness in which I could predict his fevers every month. His pediatrician insisted it was “daycare”. I knew better. After showing up with a spread sheet of fever tracking, I was able to predict the day his fever would spike. After, watching him have multiple seizures, they finally accepted my working diagnosis of PFAPA. Tonsil removal would help him out and I ultimately declined the steroids they offered. To add fuel to the fire, my father, who was extremely health conscious and fit, died after a very brief battle with prostate cancer. He wasn’t a blue light gym rat, but didn’t have the knowledge we do today in terms of circadian mismatch/arrythmia.

What was my initial approach? I read the quilt and I experimented. In my ignorance I started with food and then tackled environment. After a year of AM sardines, improved sleep and my energy retuned. My Sjogren’s reversed. I had a microdiscectomy for L4 disc herniation and central canal stenosis, which addressed my L leg radiculopathy (not so much the central back pain). I started upping my vitamin d via solar exposure and vit d supplement (not the best idea, but desperate).

I’m currently off supplements. I have a way to go, but feel 80% better. I blue block, airplane mode and minimize nnEMF when/where possible while remaining gainfully employed. Basically, when one mimic the dogs waking hours and routine and you are half way there. I read Becker’s book (fantastic). I’m currently reviewing webinars from 2016 and enjoy the dopamine spike of learning again.

My goals include:

Further hacking my immediate environment to maximize family health

Reverse fat absorption maintain weight and bring up cholesterol to normal range, along with vitamin d levels. Currently at 41. This will be a challenge with my latitude. Jet Blue points accumulating though.

Eventually integrate some of this knowledge with my patients with dementia and psychiatric illness to improve their quality of life, while staying within the guidelines of practice (which change like the wind)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-02T18:55:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/post-249971>

Too few electrons is not a problem when you have the right amount of sunlight. In fact, UVA, UVB and the VDR all slow ECT flow while promoting proton flow via the ATPase.

### kris90 — 2018-09-02T19:42:42+0300

I'm getting curious about understanding the whole concept of sunlight slowing ETC. UVA/UVB and VDR slow ETC while the 42% of red light from the sun maintains the ATPase? Essentially the work is done for us from our environment and mitochondria work less?

When UVA/UVB is low in environment at high latitudes, we need higher electron flow in mitochondria to pump protons out of the matrix to get the same effect? Mitochondria release more heat when there is a steeper temperature gradient (i.e. winter/cold) and that heat keeps the ATPase going?

### Inna — 2018-09-02T20:03:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/post-249980>

Welcome, Matt,
Am very happy to see another doc who follows Jack and understand the great value of his teaching for health. It is wonderful that you are applying Jacks protocols (connection to nature / disconnection from technology) gaining more electrons and teaching your patients to do the same. I practice pulmonary medicine, and am convinced that western medicine is doing more harm than good for health.
It is quite challenging for me to “stay within the guidelines of practice” while trying to improve quality of life for my patients.
My only hope that guidelines of practice would change with time to incorporate connection to nature disconnection from technology quantum health principles.
My mom suffered from dementia for the last 5-6 years of her life and I learned a lot while taking care of her. She was physically fairly healthy apart from dementia. We were able to keep her at her home till she died at 99 (only went to hospital for last 3 days for ‘comfort care’). I did my best to keep her away from medical establishment, as much as possible, stopped most of her meds (including) Lipitor, when she was in her 70s (probably too late).
Pfapa syndrome sound like autoimmune disease, perhaps your younger son is a canary in the mine, pointing to problem in the environment.
You might consider platinum membership with Jack, it is so worth it + you’d be supporting a great cause!
My daughter is also psychiatrist, on her last year of residency, however unlike you she is a firm believer in evidence (eminence) based medicine, not a mitochondriac. Fortunately she has my mitochondria and hence great health so far.
Best wishes for you and your family!
Inna

### Matt Resinger — 2018-09-02T21:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-too-few-electrons.21988/post-249991>

Hi Inna,

Ty for the encouragement. I am not a doctor, I am board certified as a family nurse practitioner and a psychiatric family nurse practitioner (PMHNP). I agree with everything you said. The problem here is that once you see Jack's material and learn it,...you can't un-see it. I did member up.

Your daughter will eventually come along. Once she sees the docs giving pharma talks for medications that are no better than placebo at 12 weeks (Nuplazid for example) and realizes that the only reason her colleagues are doing these pharma talks is for the $1500 speaking fee, she will come around.

As a new grad, it would be hard for your daughter to be awake yet and recognize the system we are trapped in. I think it takes a few years to notice that most of what exist within the 4 walls of the pharmacy can be quite harmful. Some exceptions include those with schizophrenia and schizoaffective disorder, for example. Outside of that, many want a quick fix without understanding the long term side effects.

### John Nicholas — 2018-09-02T20:37:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opsins-galore.21990/post-249987>

Just thinking out loud. I was sitting in post sunrise am sun. I now define this as UVA and IR light (Jansz - first question answered from my previous questions).

Given melanopsin in the eye and skin, and given UVA and IRA penetrates the skin and given the photon traps in they, would it not matter if your eyes are closed or not to obtain the same benefits of the UVA and IRA lights?

My goal is to keep my eyes open for two hours each morning. I look at the sunrise and sunset religously. After about 30 mins of being in the sunrsie (Vis + IRA), I make my breakfast. I go outside and eat in the sunlight and try to time it with UVA coming out. At times when I lay in the grass to get full exposure, I tend to naturally close my eyes and have to remind myself to open them. However, given the UVA and IRA light frequency penetration on the charts I see repeatedly in the Nourish 2017 and 2018 talks, I would think it shouldn't matter if your eyes are open or closed.

Lastly, is melanopsin the same strength in the skin as it is in the eye? Meaning, do you receive the same amount of IRA and UVA light from the skin that you do from the eye? I realize the eyes are a lot smaller than the sun. But just curious if anyone knows.

### Allin — 2018-09-02T21:24:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opsins-galore.21990/post-249990>

Dr. Kruse's orders. I have low Vitamin D for where I live (Arizona). Too much blue light (changed immediately) and I also found out I have a gene issue with my Vitamin A. He recommend, among other things I should do based on other issues i have, to watch the sunrise, the sunset and to get 2 hours of am (IR and UVA) light per day.

Today you get a special post on labor day Sept 3, 2018.......CPC#30 is now live on Patreon.

How do we feed our hungriest organ with alternative fuels? We use sweat glands to do it on our hands and feet. Other primates do not have this ability. Latitude, altitude, and population density are the keys to UV and O2. As altitude increases protons diminish in the atmosphere. At night time as magnetic flux increases there is a less positive charge (protons) in the atmosphere and with sunshine, in the daytime, there is a more positive charge in the atmosphere. The sun’s light is a cathode ray. When it hits the Earth in the daytime, the Earth acts as an anode in this cosmologic circuit and this allows for more evaporation of water on the planet's surface to create more protons to dissipate in the atmosphere. Simultaneously the evaporation effect will liberate more electrons from Earth's surface that our tensegrity system was built to be connected with to Earth's surface via our limbs. this is why humans have sweat glands on their palms and feet because we are designed to collect them as an accessory energy source for our massive brains. # Thread: New Member, Long Time Jack Kruse Listener
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-long-time-jack-kruse-listener.21997/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet

### Evan Leck — 2018-09-04T02:37:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-long-time-jack-kruse-listener.21997/post-250090>

Hi my name is Evan. I've been following Jack's work on and off for a few years now, and am now interested in really diving deeply into his work and the subject of biophysics.

I'm only 27, but I've had health issues in one way or another since I was a kid, which I attribute to being born to an older mother, having a genetic susceptibility to gluten and mold, and having an emotionally abusive dad.

I made some serious strides in my health about 2 years ago, after a particularly bad row of depression from a mold contaminated apartment I was living in. I was at my wits end from sleeping poorly. Nothing dietary wise was helping, and was drinking half a bottle of vodka per day. One day I got a random urge to look up Jack(who I was already familiar with), and came by his Self Hacked interview. I began following his recommendations: getting multiple hours of sunlight everyday, taking cold showers, and was working a job that forced me to wake up early. All of my symptoms turned around within a couple weeks.

I thought I had everything figured out, and decided to go away to grad school. However, I started suffering from severe nocturnal itching, and insomnia. I suffered through this situation for two years before I had to stop. Now, I'm trying to rebuild myself. Sadly I feel far less intelligent than the summer I before I moved away, but I think this is necessary a part of my journey, and I'm optimistic that I'll improve.

# Thread: Low iron markers – to supplement or not?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-iron-markers-%E2%80%93-to-supplement-or-not.21998/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sea Girl — 2018-09-04T14:45:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-iron-markers-%E2%80%93-to-supplement-or-not.21998/post-250122>

It seems like the area where we live has a high level of nnEMFs.

I am 37, doing the best I can in this environment. Most of my labs are pretty good, except for low vit D and low iron markers (borderline low iron and haemoglobin and very low iron saturation and ferritin). Having read all the info here about vitamin D, I understand why I have low levels despite decent sun exposure during spring, summer and autumn. So I stopped taking vit D supplements, just trying to get as much sun as possible

As for the iron, I must be getting plenty of heme form from the EpiPaleo diet with sufficient amount of grass-fed meat, liver and seafood. Apparently, my body prefers not to hold on to it. Even though my digestion is pretty normal, sometimes I take bitters or HCL to boost stomach acid (plus ginger tea and sauerkraut), but it does not make much difference.

**My question is: Can nnEMFs be the cause of poor iron absorption in the gut or impaired iron storage mechanism? Does the body try to lower the iron to make it less magnetic (or for any other reason)?**

Allopathic and functional docs were telling me to take 50-100mg of supplemental iron daily. I did it for 3 years, as a result my ferritin went from 5 to 20 (which is far from optimal), iron and saturation were quite high). At the same time my WBC (mainly neutrophils) dropped from 4.5 to 3.2, I had more tension headaches, occasional liver pain and heavier periods (I suppose, my body was just dumping all the iron).

For the last few months (after realising what impact can nnEMFs have on us) I stopped taking iron supplements. I have less headaches, but more fatigue, muscle pain and very bad recovery after exercise. I know we have to fix our environment and move to a place with less nnEMFs. But while we are still deciding and staying here, I am wondering **if I should take any iron supplements at all or just focus on iron rich food and getting enough sun exposure (as JK recommends doing to anaemic people).**

Once we move to a better place I really want to see my iron store go up as we would like to have more kids and it would be good to have higher ferritin before pregnancy. **What would you consider optimal pre-conception level?**

**Is there anyone who has fixed their iron deficiency by moving to the low nnEMF environment?**

### Michael CULLEN — 2018-09-04T15:11:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-iron-markers-%E2%80%93-to-supplement-or-not.21998/post-250124>

Never supplement Iron. Only a complete brain dead moron would tell another to take iron tablets.

Low Iron really means low Copper. See Morley robbins work. You need to support Ceruloplasmin production.

### Sea Girl — 2018-09-05T15:09:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-iron-markers-%E2%80%93-to-supplement-or-not.21998/post-250218>

> [Michael CULLEN said:](/goto/post?id=250124)
>
> Never supplement Iron. Only a complete brain dead moron would tell another to take iron tablets.
>
> Low Iron really means low Copper. See Morley robbins work. You need to support Ceruloplasmin production.

Thank you for your reply.

I'd go the other way.......only a brain dead person would listen to Morley. He misses a lot. I would not supplement iron either but the irnon story is linked to poor RBC circadian function. This smells of a melanopsin dysfunction is because of how iron is handled in humans. [Heme](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hematologist)/onc docs have been especially interested in systemic iron [metabolism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metabolism) in the diseases they treat because iron is essential for [red blood cells](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Red_blood_cells), where **MOST** of the human body's iron is contained.

So when you know where most of the iron should be and where it should not be........you are lead to getting a deeper understanding of what controls heme synthesis and IRON. And that answer shocks small thinkers like Morley. The answer is the circadian biology of mitochondria.

Sunlight stimulates RBC creation and drives the flow of iron in your body so it too is a melanopsin issue. WHY?

Heme synthesis begins its first step in mitochondria. If you have a circadian mismatch disease tied to melanopsin dysfunction by definition you are blue light toxic or nnEMF toxic and this cause a defect in mitophagy and ruins the iron cycle in humans.

People in healthcare often forget heme synthesis occurs partly in the mitochondria and partly in the cytoplasm. This is exactly how the TCA and urea cycle are built in us too. The process begins in the mitochondria because one of the precursors is found only there. Since this reaction is regulated in part by the concentration of heme, the final step (which produces the heme) is also mitochondrial.

People with defects in iron always have a blood dyscrasia at some level and it usually bleeds into fertility and thyroid hormone creation too because of its link to AM sunlight deficiency.

Remember purple light = UV light.

Purple light in sunlight stimulates RBC synthesis in humans. Blue light cause RBC's to break down sooner and live a shorter life than they can. What is the color of the main breakdown color of heme? It is the complementary yellow color, of bilirubin. Yellow is the complement of purple and orange is the complement of blue in the color wheel.

This is also why people with iron issues often have B12 and folate issues and most small thinker blame this on methylation problems but they forget that methylation is also a circadian controlled process. So.......mst people with RBC issues also have methylation defects.

UV light is purple in the visible spectrum so anyone who is anemic is deficient in SUN redox. This is also why B12 and folate absoption spectraare yellow too and are both linked to poor solar redox. Also, recall from Fritz Popp work every cell releases ELF-UV in a quantized fashion. People with bad redox always emit more UV than they absorb and they usually have iron metabolism issues that manifest in their blood. This is why I wrote the recent patreon blog about looking at your peripheral blood smear to see if you are nnEMF toxic. Once you know where the piece fits the story begins to make a lot of sense.......both quantum and common sense. [#melanopsinwisdom](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/melanopsinwisdom?hc_location=ufi) 101.

Photolysis of folate by the sun and its main serum form of 5-methylhydrofolate is BELiEVED TO be caused by UV radiation and by reactive oxygen species generated by UVA. The problem with this theory advanced by Jablonski 15 years ago is that blue hazard induces the MOST ROS in the human skin, not UV radiation as this picture shows. Note when you get to the UVA blue light transition the BLH correction drops all the way to 0.100 and is DROPPING not rising as we head into the UV range. The melanopsin paper of Dec 2017 advances Jablonski's idea that light can and does cause folate defects and can lead to neural tube defects in live births. As a neurosurgeon anytime I have asked a mother and father of a kid with a neural tube defect I always fine both parents have lived a BRISK blue light life especially at night. Second most common is having technology in their life. Anemia is present in 80% of the cases, especially during pregnancies when the woman body is further stressed. I believe this iron issue is linked to why autism shows up in first-born mles more than any other birth sequence too. Autism also shows a solar defect predilection (low D3).

Blue light melanopsin damage, not UV radiation, in my opinion, cause a serious competitive inhibition for folate in the body between the needs for cell division, DNA repair, and melanogenesis and children born with jaundice because of their parents BLH.

Since Jablonski's paper in 2002, we now know that blue is far more stimulatory to melanogenesis than UV light is. I covered that in my blog in the Time series called "When September ends". Guys like Morley have no clue about these things. This quantum data all implies the cells of humans are under a chronic severe stress response that drives the entire process and iron metabolism defects are collateral damage effects due to the mitochondrial damage in heme synethesis of RBCs is no longer controlled with a high fidelity solar controller.
